chapterbody 56: Vajrapani Bodhisattva

chapterbody: ajrapani Bodhisattva once appeared before me standing atop a lotus in the middle of a blazing fire. He was holding a curved
chapterbody 2: knife in one hand and a skull cup in the other.
chapterbody 4: Vajrapani Bodhisattva spoke to me, “Grand Master Lu, why don’t you write about the magical power of your hands?”
chapterbody 8: Vajrapani Bodhisattva replied, “There are great seals imprinted on your hands, don’t you know that?”
chapterbody 10: I said, “I would appreciate it if you explained that to me.”
chapterbody 14: “On your hands are the following markings:
chapterbody 17: 2. Dharma Vessel - Power of Deliverance
chapterbody 18: 3. Merit - Power of Pacification of Disasters
chapterbody 19: 4. Ghost King - Power of Subjugation
chapterbody 20: 5. Fortune and Virtue - Power of Enhancement
chapterbody 21: 6. Dharma Eyes - Power of Wisdom
chapterbody 22: 7. Love and Affection - Power of Love and Respect”
chapterbody 24: I was speechless.
chapterbody 26: Vajrapani Bodhisattva continued, “Grand Master Lu, you just need to lay your hand on the head of any sentient being, and dharma power will emerge. That kind of power is very inconceivable and extraordinary. You have to write this down in a book!”
chapterbody 28: I said, “I will keep this in mind!”
chapterbody 30: Vajrapani Bodhisattva said, “On one occasion, you entered into deep meditation and someone took a photograph of you. In that picture, your hands were transparent and radiating a great white light. The picture only showed your body; both hands had disappeared. Grand Master Lu, don’t you know? Your invisible hands transformed into a dharma vessel to guide and carry the spirits to the Western Pure Land of Ultimate Bliss.”
chapterbody 32: Vajrapani Bodhisattva analyzed my hands for me:
chapterbody 34: “Hands are made up of skin, nails, handprints, bones, flesh, blood vessels, and blood. They do not exist as separate entities from the rest of the body and are always undergoing subtle transformation. Their colors, textures, age, lines, and shapes change day to day. They do not remain the same and are also impermanent.”
chapterbody 36: Vajrapani Bodhisattva added,
chapterbody 38: “Hands are an extension of the body and just like the rest of the body, are made up of the elements of earth, water, fire, and wind. Hands are therefore also empty. One day, the hands will also die. At the time of dispersal of the four great elements, only space remains. We practitioners have to recognize the true reality of the emptiness of the self.”
chapterbody 40: Vajrapani Bodhisattva said finally,
chapterbody 42: “Due to the dharma power of cultivation, Grand Master Lu is able to emit infinite light from his hands. When this light is instilled into the body-mind of students, it will result in many inconceivable events. The recording of these occurrences into a book will benefit sentient beings living in the world.”
chapterbody 44: I said, “Very well! Very well! Everything is understood!”
chapterbody 46: This book has been written upon the entreaty of Vajrapani Bodhisattva. Vajrapani literally means “one with lightning in his hands.”
chapterbody 48: May this book bring benefit to everyone who reads it.

chaptername: 001 A Mark Made by the Bed Mother
chapterbody: couple brought a little boy to see me. More than half of this little boy’s face was black in color. The right corner of his mouth, to the right brow, all the way to his ear, was completely black. It was a shocking sight. He looked a little like the Black and White Judges of City God Temples.
chapterbody 3: This couple said, “When he was born, half his face was black. Some people say it was a mark made by the Bed Mother (a Taiwanese folk goddess), a birthmark. When birthmarks are located on other parts of the body, like on the buttock, they don’t have any visual impact. But, with such a mark on his face, everyone who sees him will find him strange.”
chapterbody 5: They said, “We have heard that Grand Master Lu’s hands have dharma power. We hope you can cure him!”
chapterbody 7: I said, “Why don’t you take him to see a doctor?”
chapterbody 9: They replied, “The dermatologists said there was nothing they could do about it.”
chapterbody 11: I visualized my Yidam (Personal Deity) residing on my head and the cosmic energy from the deity flowed into my body. I touched my hand upon the little boy’s face. I said, “He will be well!”
chapterbody 13: The couple left with their son. After a while, the three of them returned to see me. The parents said, “The black on his face has not faded at all. Grand Master’s hand did not seem to do him any good.”
chapterbody 15: I was quite embarrassed.
chapterbody 17: I touched his face again. This time a swirl of light appeared on my palm. Inside the light was a woman lying in a coffin. Her right cheek was immersed in water, and I finally understood.
chapterbody 19: I explained to them, “There is a problem with the feng shui of your ancestor’s burial site. It has to do with a female ancestor. Her right cheek is immersed in water, and that is the cause of the little boy’s face turning black.”
chapterbody 21: The couple said, “That must be his grandmother’s gravesite.”
chapterbody 23: They followed my instructions and had the grandmother’s body exhumed. When they opened her coffin, everyone present was stunned. The right side of the coffin was flooded, and the right side of the grandmother’s face was immersed in water and had turned black.
chapterbody 25: The couple proceeded to have the body cremated and the ashes interred in a columbarium.
chapterbody 27: After taking care of this business, the couple brought the little boy to see me again. They said, “Grand Master Lu, the reading of your palm light was so accurate. Now, what should we do?”
chapterbody 29: I stroked the little boy’s face with my hand the same way. A strange thing happened this time! After one stroke, the blackness faded a little. With another stroke, it faded even more. With several more strokes, the blackness faded further.
chapterbody 31: Finally, the little boy’s face completely turned fair.
chapterbody 33: Oh heavens!
chapterbody 35: The face of the Black and White Judge became completely normal. Many people knew of this incident. The magical hands of Sheng-yen Lu stroked the black and white face of a little boy and cured him.
chapterbody 37: Many people have come and asked me to touch their faces:
chapterbody 39: Those with crooked mouths and slanted eyes,
chapterbody 40: Those with facial nerve paralysis,
chapterbody 41: Those with black facial spots,
chapterbody 42: Those with asymmetric eyes,
chapterbody 43: Those with small noses,
chapterbody 44: Those with jug ears,
chapterbody 45: Those with many moles,
chapterbody 46: Those with many wrinkles….
chapterbody 48: They all want me to touch their faces, as if I were a cosmetic surgeon. I would like to tell everyone, “I am already seventy. I also have many wrinkles and some black spots on my face. These are natural biological phenomena. Please take note and do not make too much association. The power of Buddhadharma is infinite. But, it does not cure every ailment.
chapterbody 50: Every time I perform mo-ding (touching someone’s head), inconceivable empowerment occurs! But, this does not mean that each of the hundreds of thousands who are touched will be cured of illness with one mo-ding.
chapterbody 55: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 57: She woke up from the dream and sighed, “So this is it. It is all just a dream.”

chapterbody: depressed looking young woman sought me out at the Lei Tsang Temple. She had given birth to a daughter and had been extremely happy about it. The little girl was sweet and cute, and brought laughter and joy to the whole family. Everyone adored her.
chapterbody 3: However, around the age of three and a half, the little girl caught influenza and died. After that point, melancholia descended upon the entire family and everyone was grief stricken, especially this young woman. She had lost the darling of her life, and it was as if all happiness had been snatched from her. She was depressed and cried daily. Life was simply so unbearable that she contemplated committing suicide.
chapterbody 5: After hearing her story, I did not say anything, because all words of consolation had already been spoken by others.
chapterbody 7: I gave her a mo-ding blessing.
chapterbody 9: That night, the young woman had a dream. In her dream, she saw her daughter alive, just as cute and sweet as before. Mother and daughter were affectionate to each other and were having a great time together.
chapterbody 11: But, as soon as she woke up, she realized it was a dream and sighed,
chapterbody 13: “It was but a dream!”
chapterbody 15: The young woman came a second time to see me at the Lei Tsang Temple. I gave her another mo-ding blessing.
chapterbody 17: She had a second dream! This time, her daughter was several years older and was at the top of all her classes at school. The mother was very happy. Mother and daughter were of one mind, and there was nothing that they would not talk about together. The daughter was elected class monitor and a model student. An entire wall at home was covered with her awards. Her mother was elated when people complimented her for having such an excellent daughter.
chapterbody 19: She woke up and had the realization: “It was but a dream!”
chapterbody 21: The young woman came to see me again at the Lei Tsang Temple, and I again gave her a mo-ding.
chapterbody 23: This time, the dream was even more amazing.
chapterbody 25: In this dream, her daughter graduated from college and went overseas to further her studies. After obtaining a Master’s degree, she went on to receive a PhD degree. Then she found a very good job abroad, and everything was smooth sailing for this daughter. Although they missed each other very much, there were very few opportunities for mother and daughter to see each other. Later, her daughter married and decided to settle down in the foreign country. With a job and a family, the times she returned home to visit were even fewer. She would occasionally contact her mother.
chapterbody 27: In the dream, the young woman caught a glimpse of herself in a mirror and found herself already white-haired.
chapterbody 29: She woke up from the dream and sighed, “So this is it. It is all just a dream.”
chapterbody 31: The young woman came a fourth time to see me at the Lei Tsang Temple.
chapterbody 33: I asked her, “Do you want another mo-ding?”
chapterbody 35: She replied, “No! I already know the result.”
chapterbody 37: I asked her, “What is the result?”
chapterbody 39: She replied, “How long you spend together with someone is simply an interlude of affinity. In life, all affinities end in death or separation. Just regard it all as a dream. When one wakes up from the dream, everything is released.”
chapterbody 41: The young woman thanked me for giving her mo-ding. She was completely cured of her depression.
chapterbody 43: I never tried to console her. I simply gave her mo-ding. And she was able to attain this realization.
chapterbody 47: She stood in the crowd, and when Grand Master Lu walked by, he only lightly tapped her head without saying anything. The mo-ding was over just like that.
chapterbody 49: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: n Southeast Asia, a woman in her fifties attended group practices at a True Buddha School local chapter through the introduction of friends. Soon she took refuge in Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu.
chapterbody 3: This woman had a chronic illness.
chapterbody 5: This is her story. When she was in her twenties, she had a slender figure and angelic features. Her beauty charmed everyone. A bully from her village fell in love with her. This bully was also a sociopath who had engaged in all kinds of criminal activities: sexually assaulting several women, intimidating and taking money from others, leading a gang which exacted protection fees, and abetting murders.
chapterbody 7: This beautiful girl did not want to be friends with the bully. She refused his courtship. When he asked her for dates, she would find excuses to decline.
chapterbody 9: Flying into a rage, the bully threatened her, “Just wait and see, you are going to regret this!”
chapterbody 11: She begged, “Don’t kill me.”
chapterbody 13: The bully said, “I will not kill you. I will make you so miserable that you wish you were dead. That will really be fun!”
chapterbody 15: She asked, “Are you going to break my arms and legs?”
chapterbody 17: The bully said, “No. Don’t you know that my father is a famous voodoo magician?”
chapterbody 19: After that conversation, the beautiful girl and the bully went their separate ways.
chapterbody 21: But, she later came down with a “headache syndrome.” This was no ordinary headache. The splitting pain was so excruciating that she felt as if her head were being chopped and she had fainted on the ground.
chapterbody 23: The young lady sought medical treatment, but no one could figure out what was wrong.
chapterbody 25: She also sought voodoo masters to break the black magic, all to no avail. Instead she developed severe insomnia.
chapterbody 27: Due to the headache syndrome, the young lady lost her beauty as her face became distorted. Her body became thickened and coarse from over consumption of pain killers. This headache syndrome had been torturing her for thirty years.
chapterbody 29: After taking refuge, she started crying and whining to Padmakumara at the shrine daily.
chapterbody 31: One day, she had a dream. Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu appeared to tell her, “Go and find Grand Master Lu to give you a mo-ding.”
chapterbody 33: Hence, she traveled with other fellow students to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple. She attended the fire offering ceremony, listened to the dharma teaching, and finally waited for her turn for Grand Master Lu’s mo-ding. The temple ground was packed with close to ten thousand people. She stood in the crowd, and when Grand Master Lu walked by, he only lightly tapped her head without saying anything. The mo-ding was over just like that.
chapterbody 35: That night in her hotel, she had another dream.
chapterbody 37: Grand Master Lu came and said to her, “I will help pull out the six iron nails.”
chapterbody 39: As soon as he finished speaking, six little black men appeared above her head.
chapterbody 41: Grand Master Lu ordered, “Why aren’t you leaving? What are you waiting for?”
chapterbody 43: The six little black men said, “We know you are formidable. But, we can’t bear to leave this residence that has been ours for thirty years!”
chapterbody 45: Grand Master became angry. His hands turned into blazing Vajra claws. The six little black men immediately ran off swiftly in fear! When the woman awoke, her headache syndrome was completely cured. Her thirty-years of headaches and insomnia were swept away. This is a living testimony.
chapterbody 47: I say:
chapterbody 49: May merits from my hands
chapterbody 50: Eradicate all illnesses and distresses
chapterbody 51: Increase all fortunes
chapterbody 52: Sow the seed of enlightenment
chapterbody 53: Prevent disasters from emerging
chapterbody 54: And eliminate all evil black magic
chapterbody 57: Bring harmony and great perfection

chapterbody 59: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: ave you ever hidden something in a safe place, then found yourself unable to recall the location after some time has lapsed?
chapterbody 3: This is especially common with the elderly. They are often forgetful. For example, one might have taken medicine just a few moments ago, however memory of that may now be blank, so that one does not know if taking of the medicine occurred.
chapterbody 5: An elderly person may have just had a meal. However, when you ask him, “Have you eaten yet?” He may reply, “Not yet!” He has already forgotten the whole thing.
chapterbody 7: Older folks may be able to recall memories from a long time ago. But, they cannot recall what happened yesterday. You may have just been introduced to them the day before, and they may now ask, “Who are you?” That memory has been swiped clean!
chapterbody 9: US President Mr. Reagan suffered from Alzheimer’s in his later years. He could not even recognize his own wife Nancy. Many elderly people with memory loss cannot find their way home unescorted.
chapterbody 11: They have forgotten their own names!
chapterbody 12: They have forgotten their family members!
chapterbody 13: They have forgotten their home address!
chapterbody 16: Families have to place ads in newspapers to look for these missing family members.
chapterbody 18: One time, during mo-ding, I placed my hand on the head of an old gentleman. He suddenly let out a loud cry, “I found it!”
chapterbody 20: I was startled. What did he find?
chapterbody 22: The old gentleman said, “I hid in my safe cash, gold, jewelries, expensive watches, bank account passbooks, seals, and foreign currencies.”
chapterbody 24: He was afraid that someone would get hold of his safe key. So, he hid his safe key in the following places:
chapterbody 26: Under the sofa, Inside a pillow,
chapterbody 27: Behind the bookcase,
chapterbody 28: Inside the pocket of clothing in the closet,
chapterbody 29: Behind an inscribed board,
chapterbody 30: Inside a flower pot, Inside the refrigerator,
chapterbody 31: Above the ceiling,
chapterbody 32: Between a box and the mattress,
chapterbody 33: Inside an old shoe.
chapterbody 35: He would keep the key in each hiding place for a short while. Then he would move it to another location. This key was too important and he had to keep moving it.
chapterbody 37: The problem was, there were just too many hiding places. As time went by, he found that he had forgotten the hiding place. He started looking for the missing key and after searching everywhere, he still could not find it. He was in great dismay.
chapterbody 39: The old man came to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple and asked me to give him mo-ding. When I touched his head, as if there were a flash of lightning, he saw in his mind: “Air-conditioning vent!”
chapterbody 41: Oh heavens! Once Grand Master conferred mo-ding, the old gentleman saw the “air-conditioning vent.”
chapterbody 43: He went home to look. The key indeed was sitting squarely inside the air-conditioning vent!
chapterbody 45: The old man said, “Thank you, Grand Master Lu!”
chapterbody 47: This old man comes frequently to seek mo-ding, because he is able to recover a little more of his lost memory with each mo-ding. After many instances of mo-ding, he now has normal recollection.
chapterbody 49: The old man said, “This is indeed very marvelous!”
chapterbody 53: The man was worried that the green tree viper would slither back and re-enter his arm. So he beseeched Grand Master Lu to quickly conduct a bardo deliverance for the snake.
chapterbody 54: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: hen I confer mo-ding, I always visualize my Yidam residing atop my head and myself transforming into the Yidam.
chapterbody 3: While moving my hand from one person’s head to the next, I chant the Yidam’s mantra simultaneously.
chapterbody 5: My hands are moving very fast. Otherwise, the whole process may take as long as one or two hours.
chapterbody 7: Once, after I placed my hand on a man’s head, he said to me, “Grand Master Lu, my right arm is very painful. It has been hurting for half a year. Please help me by giving it a pat.”
chapterbody 9: When I heard that, I immediately used my right hand to pat his right arm from shoulder to hand. I patted the three joints: upper arm joint, elbow joint, and wrist joint.
chapterbody 11: When finished, I continued giving mo-ding to other people. A week later, we heard back from the man:
chapterbody 13: After he received the patting, his arm was still in excruciating pain for about two days. Two days later, he had a dream.
chapterbody 15: In the dream, he saw a snake sliding out of his arm. It was a long and slender Chinese Green Tree Viper. Flicking its tongue rapidly, the snake slithered away.
chapterbody 17: When he awoke, this man noticed that his arm had stopped hurting. Before, when his arm was in pain, he could neither raise his arm nor bend his elbow. He also could not maneuver his fingers to do any writing.
chapterbody 19: After I patted his arm, he was able to raise his arm, bend his elbow, and write. Everything has returned to normal. Hooray! Hooray!
chapterbody 21: This is the story told by the man. He lives in Mingjian Township and is a tea farmer. His farm produces high quality teas. He also owns another plot of land where he cultivates mulberries.
chapterbody 23: About half a year ago, he was taking an afternoon nap under a mulberry tree. Suddenly he felt something moving on his belly and he woke up startled. When he opened his eyes, he saw a slender green tree viper crawling on his belly.
chapterbody 25: Instinctively he grabbed it. The snake readily turned its head and was about to bite him. He was still holding in his right hand the farm tool he had been working with earlier. With a wave of his sickle, the snake was cut into two pieces.
chapterbody 27: This incident did not worry him at all. After all, as someone who has been working around farms, he has seen many snakes before:
chapterbody 29: Chinese Green Tree Vipers,
chapterbody 31: Elaphe snakes,
chapterbody 32: Taiwan Habu snakes,
chapterbody 33: Pythons,
chapterbody 35: Water snakes,
chapterbody 36: Etc.
chapterbody 38: That was why he did not give this incident another thought. But, after Grand Master Lu patted his painful arm and he subsequently had the dream, he remembered this incident. This was also a coincidence.
chapterbody 40: The man was worried that the green tree viper would slither back
chapterbody 41: and re-enter his arm. So he beseeched Grand Master Lu to quickly conduct a bardo deliverance for the snake.
chapterbody 43: Therefore, I carried out his wish and delivered the spirit of the green tree viper.
chapterbody 45: I thought that would be the end. Unexpectedly the story continued. After I performed the deliverance, the man had another dream. He again dreamt of the green tree viper, but this time it extended its dragon claws, scales grew out of its body, and its head transformed into a dragon head.
chapterbody 47: After saying “thank you” to the man, it soared up and flew towards the clouds!
chapterbody 49: This is a good dream!
chapterbody 53: This was exactly the same house Grand Master Lu had shown him in his dream.
chapterbody 54: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: he assistant manager of a company wanted to find the right home for his family of five. He was married and had three children.
chapterbody 3: So in early 2012, he asked a real estate agent to start looking for a house. Every Saturday, Sunday, even on his days off, sometimes even in the evening, he would look at houses. He was very active in this pursuit.
chapterbody 4: Helping and saving sentient beings is what a practitioner should do - this is Mahayana.
chapterbody 5: The assistant manager had the following requirements:
chapterbody 7: He wished for a home with four bedrooms, one living room, one kitchen, three bathrooms, and one room to be a shrine room.
chapterbody 9: He wanted an optimal price, no errant feng shui elements, and clear legal rights to the property.
chapterbody 11: But, after over a year and looking at more than one hundred houses, the house he was looking for was nowhere to be found.
chapterbody 13: The assistant manager became agitated, and his real estate agent also was in a bad mood.
chapterbody 15: Some houses were good, but the feng shui was bad.
chapterbody 16: Some houses were priced too high.
chapterbody 17: Some had problems with property rights.
chapterbody 18: Some were just not what he was looking for.
chapterbody 19: Some were in complicated neighborhoods with poor environments.
chapterbody 21: There was not a single one that fit his requirements.
chapterbody 23: It got to the point that each time the assistant manager went to look at a house, he did not hold any hope. He could not figure out what the problem was.
chapterbody 25: One day, he suddenly had a strange idea. “Why don’t I ask Grand Master Lu to empower me and help me find the right house?”
chapterbody 27: Kneeling amidst the crowd, waiting for his turn to receive mo-ding, the assistant manager chanted mentally, “Please help me find the right house! Please help me find the right house! Please help me find the right house!”
chapterbody 29: I walked towards him and placed my hand on the assistant manager’s head.
chapterbody 31: Surprisingly a miracle happened. That night, Grand Master Lu came and brought him to look at a house. It was a furnished house with exactly four bedrooms, one living room, one kitchen, three bathrooms, and one of the rooms was a study. This house already had furniture and was even decorated with curtains.
chapterbody 33: Grand Master Lu and the assistant manager sat together in the living room. Grand Master Lu said, “This is your future home. It has good feng shui.”
chapterbody 35: The assistant manager looked around him:
chapterbody 36: The curtains were green like water.
chapterbody 37: The furniture was classic in style.
chapterbody 38: The sofa was purple.
chapterbody 39: In the living room was a redwood bookcase with gold trim.
chapterbody 40: A television was installed inside another redwood case.
chapterbody 42: Grand Master Lu said, “You only need to remodel the study into a shrine room.”
chapterbody 44: The assistant manager was very happy with the house. Then he woke up from the dream.
chapterbody 46: Three days later, the real estate agent brought him to see a house. The agent said to him, “This is a furnished house. The owner’s post was transferred overseas, and he is in a hurry to sell it. The price is right. Just take a look!”
chapterbody 48: When the assistant manager walked into the house, he almost jumped. This was exactly the same house Grand Master Lu had shown him in his dream.
chapterbody 50: The water colored curtains.
chapterbody 51: The purple sofa.
chapterbody 52: The bookcase and another wood case made of redwood.
chapterbody 53: A television inside the redwood case.
chapterbody 55: Oh heavens! Everything matched. How could this be possible? How could it be so accurate?
chapterbody 57: One of the four rooms was a study.
chapterbody 61: I said, “Did I? Ha ha ha!”
chapterbody 62: The assistant manager went to Lei Tsang Temple to tell me, “Thank you, Grand Master, for bestowing me an auspicious and luxurious home!”
chapterbody 63: The assistant manager replied, “In my dream, you took me to see the luxury home!”

chapterbody 2: took an overseas trip to teach the dharma. When I arrived at the local chapter, it was completely filled with people. Crowds were everywhere, inside, outside, as well as on the streets.
chapterbody 4: Suddenly, an ambulance drove towards us, its siren shrieking incessantly. When the crowds heard the siren of the ambulance, they automatically stepped aside to let the vehicle through. The ambulance traveled slowly and came to a stop in front of me.
chapterbody 8: She said to me, “Help! Help! Grand Master Lu, please help me!”
chapterbody 10: I asked, “How can I help you?”
chapterbody 12: The woman said, “For a long time I have heard of the fame of Grand Master Lu and how you have helped countless people. I hired an ambulance to take a patient from the hospital to come here and see you. Grand Master Lu, please have great compassion and save him.”
chapterbody 14: I asked, “What illness does the patient have?”
chapterbody 16: The woman replied, “He had a severe bleeding stroke two years ago and has not awakened since. The last two years, he has been in a coma. There is nothing more the doctors can do for him. He is my husband, a very honest person. I hope you can help him.”
chapterbody 18: I said, “Whether I can cure him, I dare not say. I hope a miracle happens!”
chapterbody 20: The back door of the ambulance opened. A patient was lying in a bed, hooked to oxygen, with an intravenous infusion, and a respirator. Tubes were coming out of his nose. He lay there motionless, his eyes tightly shut.
chapterbody 22: I joined my palms and prayed to my Yidam. I also brought my mind into one-pointedness to chant the heart mantra of the Yidam.
chapterbody 24: At this time, my hands automatically moved away from each other, my feet started moving in an oblique parallel step, the Bu-ding Bu-ba Stance, and my left hand and right hand held the Tarjani Mudra and Sword Mudra respectively. A stream of cosmic energy descended into my body.
chapterbody 26: The Sword Mudra drew automatically the Four Vertical-Five Horizontal Seal: four vertical and five horizontal lines, accompanied by the nine characters of: lin, bing, dou, zhe, jie, zhen, lie, zai, qian. (These nine words together mean: Soldiers at the frontline putting themselves in battle array.)
chapterbody 28: This was an empowerment of the Four Vertical-Five Horizontal Seal accompanied by the Nine True Words Mantra.
chapterbody 30: After the empowerment, I blew a breath of qi on my right palm and performed laying of the hand on the patient’s brow-point.
chapterbody 32: The flow of cosmic energy had instilled itself through my entire body. When I drew the seal, waved the mudras, and blew qi onto my hand, I could feel the presence of a very strong force. I sensed that the buddhas and bodhisattvas and Golden Mother indeed wanted to help this person.
chapterbody 34: After the ritual was complete, the ambulance put on its siren again and drove off.
chapterbody 36: At this time, I then walked into the local chapter. I ascended to the dharma seat, and gave a discourse on the importance of “harmony.”
chapterbody 38: I said, “One should not harbor any thoughts of discrimination between oneself and others. You, I, he or she, are one family. Helping and saving sentient beings is what a practitioner should do - this is Mahayana. You do not only refrain from harming others, you also have to dedicate yourself to helping others. We all are originally one indivisible Great Luminous Buddha-essence that pervades throughout the spaciousness and the phenomenal world.”
chapterbody 40: When I finished the discourse, the audience applauded.
chapterbody 42: At that moment, someone brought the news:
chapterbody 44: When the patient in the ambulance, who had been comatose for two years, returned to the hospital, he was already able to open his eyes and his mouth and was murmuring that a bodhisattva had come and saved him.
chapterbody 46: The people in the hospital exclaimed: “Miracle! Miracle!”
chapterbody 48: People in the local chapter also cried out: “This is just too inconceivable!”
chapterbody 50: I said, “When people are sincere, buddhas will definitely respond!”
chapterbody 55: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 57: I did not answer him right away. I thought for a moment and said, “I will give you dream guidance!”

chapterbody: his is a gambling story. The goal of this essay is not to promote gambling, but rather to demonstrate simply the “magical power of mo-ding.”
chapterbody 3: I personally do not advocate gambling, so please do not emulate what I describe below.
chapterbody 5: In 2013, between March and May, North Korea and South Korea entered into a dispute. A student came excitedly to ask me, “Will North Korea attack South Korea?”
chapterbody 7: I did not answer him right away. I thought for a moment and said, “I will give you dream guidance!” I then gave this questioning student mo-ding, so that he would receive an answer in his dreams.
chapterbody 9: My own thoughts about the situation are:
chapterbody 11: 1. North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, said he would resort to war to solve everything.
chapterbody 12: 2. TV anchors in North Korea announced to the world that the country was armed and ready to go to war and that they would definitely crush their enemies.
chapterbody 13: 3. Military chiefs in North Korea demonstrated their determination to go to war, not just with verbal threats, but by repeatedly deploying troops.
chapterbody 14: 4. North Korea had moved two short-range missile launchers to its east coast.
chapterbody 15: 5. North Korea recalled North Korean workers from the joint (Kaesong) Industrial Park.
chapterbody 16: 6. North Korea asked foreign embassies to consider evacuation as their safety could not be guaranteed.
chapterbody 17: 7. North Korea had prepared rockets on standby for firing at Okinawa, Guam, Japan, South Korea, and even the United States.
chapterbody 18: 8. Amid growing tension, the entire world had focused their attention on South and North Korea.
chapterbody 22: The TV anchors said, “War!”
chapterbody 24: Military chiefs said, “War!”
chapterbody 26: I thought to myself, “Perhaps they will indeed go to war!”
chapterbody 28: But that student went home and had this dream: I appeared to him and said, “From March till May 1st, the Koreans will not go to war!”
chapterbody 30: He was very excited and immediately went to place a bet on “No War.”
chapterbody 32: As it turned out, someone predicted that North Korea would start a war on the birth anniversary of the late leader Kim Jong-il. Another predicted the war would start within a few days of the military exercises of South Korea and the United States.
chapterbody 34: Will there be a war? It became the focus of an international crisis.
chapterbody 36: Hence, bookmakers were accepting wagers on whether or not war would break out between the two Koreas between March to May 1st.
chapterbody 38: Let us place a wager!
chapterbody 40: People who believed there would be war placed bets on “yes.”
chapterbody 42: People who did not believe there would be war placed bets on “no.”
chapterbody 44: That student of mine also wanted to place a bet which was why he came to ask me if there would be a war.
chapterbody 46: Many believed there would be war because the crisis was marked by extreme escalation of rhetoric. Others believed such rhetoric was merely intimidation, a tiger head with a rat’s tail, or a barking dog that would not bite. Some felt the crisis was so tense that South Korea’s President Park needed to come forward to make a negotiation. Others felt North Korea had made threats against South Korea many times before, but nothing had yet resulted, so war would be unlikely.
chapterbody 48: Ultimately: “There was no war between March to May 1st.”
chapterbody 50: As a result, my student exclaimed, “I won big money! Ha ha ha!”
chapterbody 55: Sheng-yen Lu

chaptername: 009 Please Tap a Few More Times
chapterbody: n 2013, during my stay in Taiwan, every Saturday at 3 p.m., I conducted fire offerings to various deities at the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple. In the evening after dinner, I would proceed to give everyone present a mo-ding.
chapterbody 3: Every Saturday, close to ten thousand people attended the fire ceremony. The following verses serve as a testimony:
chapterbody 5: Mo-ding by Buddha’s hands brings great joy,
chapterbody 7: Pacification, enhancement, magnetization, and subjugation are fulfilled,
chapterbody 8: Miracles are realized again and again.
chapterbody 10: My bestowal of mo-ding has brought about many remarkable and wonderful happenings.
chapterbody 12: One incident occurred to someone who is a manufacturer of precision machinery. He exports machinery mainly to Europe, America, and Japan.
chapterbody 14: In good economic times, purchase orders flew in continuously. Even during the financial tsunami, his orders still trickled in.
chapterbody 16: This was because the machines designed by him were of excellent quality and highly reputable. Although other manufacturers had no orders, he still had a stable business.
chapterbody 18: He made a lot of money. He purchased lands. He built tall buildings. He made investments.
chapterbody 20: But, in the second half of 2012, a strange thing occurred. His business came to a standstill. During this period, he did not receive a single order. Such a thing had never happened before.
chapterbody 22: In the machinery industry, many companies had collapsed.
chapterbody 24: And for him, there was not a single purchase order. He became very anxious. With no orders, how could he maintain his business?
chapterbody 26: He heard about the efficacy of Grand Master Lu’s mo-ding and decided to approach the temple to beseech a mo-ding.
chapterbody 28: Strangely, after receiving his first mo-ding, he received a purchase order the next day.
chapterbody 30: He attended another ceremony for another mo-ding, and he received a second purchase order. Coincidentally, this order was received the day following this mo-ding.
chapterbody 32: By now he had faith, and he went to the temple for a third time. Similarly he received a purchase order the following day.
chapterbody 34: At this point, he had received three purchase orders.
chapterbody 36: The following week, he was very busy and he did not go to the temple to attend fire offering nor the mo-ding ritual. There was no purchase order the next day. He did not get any orders the rest of the week.
chapterbody 38: So he went to the temple again. And indeed the next day he received a purchase order.
chapterbody 40: This has proven effective every time. It was very strange.
chapterbody 42: One time, I walked by this chairman. He called out, “Grand Master Lu, please tap a few more times!”
chapterbody 44: I turned around and tapped him three times.
chapterbody 46: Another strange thing happened. The next day he received three purchase orders.
chapterbody 48: For a period of time, Taiwan’s export was in a slump. But, with Grand Master Lu’s mo-ding, he was able to keep his business going.
chapterbody 50: This chairman came especially to see me and tell me about his experiences. He also requested, “Grand Master Lu, please tap a few more times!”
chapterbody 52: I asked him, “Is it really this efficacious?”
chapterbody 57: The chairman replied, “Indeed! Without mo-ding, I had no orders. After mo-ding, an order came in. With three taps, came three orders.”
chapterbody 62: A stream of cosmic energy entered the baby’s body, rushing throughout his body from his brain.

chapterbody: couple with a little baby flew four hours to see me at the Ling Shen Ching Tze Temple in Redmond. As soon as they debarked from the airplane, they hurried to the temple to meet with me.
chapterbody 3: Upon their arrival, the person in charge asked, “Why do you want to see Grand Master Lu?”
chapterbody 5: The couple replied, “It is a matter of utmost importance!”
chapterbody 7: The person in charge laughed, “Everyone who wants to see Grand Master Lu says the same thing. What actually is the matter?”
chapterbody 9: The couple said, “It has to do with our baby. It is very urgent.”
chapterbody 11: The person in charge said, “Grand Master Lu usually comes to the temple for lunch at noon. You can wait here for a little while.”
chapterbody 13: The couple spoke impatiently, “We will go to Grand Master Lu’s residence then.”
chapterbody 15: The person in charge said, “Grand Master Lu’s residence address is a secret. We do not know it either.”
chapterbody 17: When the husband heard that, he became angry and said, “This is outrageous.”
chapterbody 19: In an agitated state, the couple holding the baby went around asking everyone for Grand Master Lu and received the same answer.
chapterbody 21: Finally noon arrived and I met with them and learned the following story:
chapterbody 23: The wife had given birth to a baby boy. During delivery, the umbilical cord became entwined around his neck. For a period of time, the little baby was unable to breath, resulting in hypoxia to his brain.
chapterbody 25: At birth, the little baby had the following symptoms:
chapterbody 27: 1. A darkened face.
chapterbody 28: 2. A sunken depression on the skull.
chapterbody 29: 3. Eyes not opening.
chapterbody 31: 5. Slowness.
chapterbody 32: 6. The limbs not responding.
chapterbody 33: 7. Vomiting.
chapterbody 35: It was a medical emergency. Since the infant was demonstrating all these abnormal conditions, the hospital put him inside an incubator for observation.
chapterbody 37: The couple asked the doctor, “Can he be saved?”
chapterbody 39: “We are not sure,” the doctor replied. “He may be crippled even if he survives!”
chapterbody 41: Oh heavens! The couple started crying aloud.
chapterbody 43: A little baby’s life was hanging by a thread of hope.
chapterbody 45: The baby survived, but there was no improvement in his symptoms. They asked the doctor again. The doctor opened his hands, signifying helplessness.
chapterbody 47: At that time, someone told them, “Why don’t you fly to Seattle to ask for help from Grand Master Lu at the Ling Shen Ching Tze Temple?”
chapterbody 49: “Will it help?”
chapterbody 51: The person said, “Grand Master Lu is miraculous! You have to ask him to lay his hand on the baby’s entire body.”
chapterbody 53: So the couple brought their little baby and his oxygen tank to see me.
chapterbody 54: I placed my hand on the head of the little baby. I then touched his eyes, ears, mouth, and nose.
chapterbody 56: A stream of cosmic energy entered the baby’s body, rushing throughout his body from his brain.
chapterbody 58: Unexpectedly, the little baby opened his mouth and spit out several pieces of “darkish blood.”
chapterbody 59: Upon seeing this, the couple became afraid.
chapterbody 60: Later on, the baby slept very soundly with normal breathing. When he woke up, his face was blushing, his brain was normal, and he opened both eyes. He was able to hear sounds and move his limbs. He opened his mouth making noises.
chapterbody 66: Wow! What a wonderful thing! The couple happily took refuge in the True Buddha!
chapterbody 68: To me, Christianity is a stage along the heavenly pathway of Buddhism.

chapterbody: woman asked me to bless the photograph of a sick man. The person in the picture was in his thirties.
chapterbody 3: The woman said, “This is my nephew. He lives in Australia and is an electrical engineer. Last year he was diagnosed with cancer and underwent chemotherapy. But now the cancer cells have spread throughout his body. Grand Master Lu, please bless him and see if he will get well?”
chapterbody 5: I put my hand upon the photograph.
chapterbody 7: I visualized my Yidam with my eyes closed. What I saw instead was the image of Jesus Christ.
chapterbody 9: The image of Jesus was just like the one we normally see in pictures of him:
chapterbody 11: Long hair and long beard,
chapterbody 13: Both eyes full of compassion and tenderness,
chapterbody 15: Dressed like a lama wearing inner white and outer red garments, Hands extending obliquely,
chapterbody 17: Light radiating from his entire body.
chapterbody 19: Jesus descended slowly from the clouds and took the man in the picture away.
chapterbody 21: Seeing this with my own eyes, I was quite surprised!
chapterbody 23: I said, “He has died, and Jesus came to escort him away.”
chapterbody 25: The woman said, “Grand Master Lu, you must not make irresponsible remarks. I just contacted him yesterday, and he agreed to have me bring his picture to you for a blessing!”
chapterbody 27: I repeated, “He has died, and it was indeed Jesus who came to escort him.”
chapterbody 29: The woman said, “He is not a Christian. Early on, he followed me to take refuge in you, Grand Master Lu!”
chapterbody 31: At that time, the woman took out her cell phone to make a call to the gentleman’s mother in Australia. As the conversation progressed, a stupefied look appeared on her face!
chapterbody 33: When she hung up, she turned to me, “Grand Master Lu, I am in awe!”
chapterbody 35: I asked, “Why?”
chapterbody 37: The woman said, “At the time you put your hand upon his photograph, he passed away! It happened right at that time.”
chapterbody 39: The woman continued, “My nephew had originally taken refuge in you. Three years ago, he met a Caucasian girl and became close to her. The girl is Christian, and she took my nephew to church to get baptized. Hanging in my nephew’s home now is a huge picture of Jesus.”
chapterbody 41: I said, “That is very good!”
chapterbody 43: The woman said, “So it turned out that it was Jesus who came to escort him. Your description of Jesus matches the same image of Jesus in the huge picture.”
chapterbody 45: I said, “Amen!”
chapterbody 47: The woman asked, “Grand Master Lu, you do not reject Christianity?”
chapterbody 49: I replied, “Of course not.”
chapterbody 51: The woman asked, “Why?”
chapterbody 53: I explained, “I was once a Christian. I also taught Sunday school and served as a Bible study teacher. Later due to a mystical encounter I developed faith in Taoism and Buddhism. To me, Christianity is a stage along the heavenly pathway of Buddhism. I am an enlightened master who has seen the true nature of reality. I do not discriminate. What I have is a mind of equanimity.”
chapterbody 56: You are of the innate essence of the white lotus, therefore you are inseparable from the White Lotus King as well as inseparable from Atisa.
chapterbody 58: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: ne morning in May 2013, I saw a sun disc arising in the middle of a large pine tree. Inside the sun disc was a person.
chapterbody 2: This dazzling person was sitting atop a radiant lotus light inside the sun disc. Radiating from him were:
chapterbody 5: Wheel Spoke lights,
chapterbody 7: Dignified Subtle lights
chapterbody 9: When I saw this, I was overjoyed. But, I did not know who this venerable person was. So my right hand formed the Hook Invocation Mudra and the left the Hour Mudra. (I learned this mudra from deities who are responsible for daily official duties. By pressing a spot on the hand corresponding to the current hour, one can invoke various deities to descend. The secret has to do with the amazing seed syllable Mo.)
chapterbody 11: Indeed the person in the sun disc descended.
chapterbody 13: I asked, “Venerable One, who are you?”
chapterbody 15: He replied, “I am Serlingpa.”
chapterbody 17: I asked, “I have been to Jambi, Indonesia, the place where Atisa had sought out Serlingpa to teach him the dharma. Upon my arrival, thunderclaps erupted in the sky, drops of rain started falling, and a great trembling went through my heart. Why did this happen?”
chapterbody 19: Serlingpa replied, “That is always the case when people of deep affinity come to visit!”
chapterbody 21: I asked, “Master Serlingpa, can you elucidate a puzzle in my mind?”
chapterbody 23: Serlingpa replied, “It is because of this puzzle that I have appeared. Do you want to ask me about the origin of my lineage?”
chapterbody 25: I replied, “That is correct.”
chapterbody 27: Hence Serlingpa said, “I was born on the Island of Java. At that time the teaching of the Buddha greatly flourished on the island. Shakyamuni Buddha transmitted his lineage to Mahakasyapa, Mahakasyapa passed it to Ananda, and later it was passed down to Maha Kusulupa who passed it to Kusulupa, and Kusulupa to Serlingpa. This is the origin of my lineage.”
chapterbody 29: I asked, “People of the world only know you as the master from Suvarnadvipi. Do you have other names?”
chapterbody 31: Serlingpa replied, “I was born in Java, named Bian-dou (phonetic rendition). Later my dharma name became the White Lotus King of Great Innate Essence.”
chapterbody 33: When I heard that, I was amazed! “White Lotus King!”
chapterbody 35: I am in this life the incarnation of the White Mahapadmakumara. Serlingpa is the White Lotus King. What is the connection?
chapterbody 37: I asked, “Who is Atisa then?”
chapterbody 39: Serlingpa replied, “When Atisa came to visit me in Suvarnadvipi, I transmitted everything I knew to him. Because Atisa and I have been in a teacher-student relationship for many lifetimes, there is no separation between the two.”
chapterbody 41: I asked, “What about me, Grand Master Lu?”
chapterbody 43: Serlingpa replied, “You are of the innate essence of the white lotus, therefore you are inseparable from the White Lotus King as well as inseparable from Atisa.
chapterbody 45: That was why there were thunderclaps from the sky when you arrived at Jambi.”
chapterbody 47: I asked, “Inseparable innate essence? I don’t understand.” Serlingpa explained, “The Buddha has said - everything is of one integrated aspect. If you understand one integrated aspect, you will understand everything!”
chapterbody 51: I also visualized the kidney stones turning into emptiness.

chapterbody: was administering mo-ding between two long queues of people. There were really many people.
chapterbody 3: A gentleman hurried towards me shouting at the same time, “Please excuse me. Let me through.”
chapterbody 5: People replied, “We are all waiting in line. You also need to wait in line. Just go to end of the line!”
chapterbody 7: The man said, “I have kidney stones, and I am in great pain now. I cannot wait. Please help me. Grand Master Lu, please help me. Come on! Come on!”
chapterbody 9: I raised my head to take a look at the man. Beads of perspiration glistened on his brow. He was shouting, “It hurts, it hurts, it hurts.”
chapterbody 11: I walked out of the crowd, and I started chanting:
chapterbody 13: Heaven is round and earth square,
chapterbody 14: Nine codes of law and ethics,
chapterbody 15: I now cast the spell,
chapterbody 18: I lightly tapped the man’s back over his kidneys. And I chanted the mantra: Om, si-ba-wa, su-da, sa-er-wa, da-er-ma, si-ba-wa, su-do-hang.” (This is the Emptiness Mantra. I also visualized the kidney stones turning into emptiness.)
chapterbody 20: After tapping him a few times, the gentleman suddenly stood up and said, “It doesn’t hurt anymore! The pain is gone! Thank you! Thank you!”
chapterbody 22: When the crowd witnessed this, everyone was wowed.
chapterbody 24: Even stranger, that same night, after this man returned home, he had a dream. Several people suddenly walked into his room and called out, “Surgery! Surgery!”
chapterbody 26: The man said, “I don’t want surgery!”
chapterbody 28: Those people said, “Grand Master Lu has tapped your kidneys a few times to stop the pain, but it was just symptomatic relief. You will still have recurrent pain in the future. We are celestial doctors coming to perform surgery on you.”
chapterbody 30: The man said, “I only believe in Grand Master Lu!”
chapterbody 32: Those people said, “It is because of your belief in Grand Master Lu that we have come.”
chapterbody 34: Without further explanation, the celestial doctors surrounded the man and started working.
chapterbody 36: One opened his abdomen.
chapterbody 37: One took out his kidney.
chapterbody 38: One removed all the kidney stones by picking them out - pointed ones, round ones, odd-shaped ones. Then he swiped his hand over the incision of the kidney, and the kidney healed.
chapterbody 40: Another placed a hand on his abdomen, and the incision wound also healed immediately.
chapterbody 42: The man saw these surgical procedures vividly, but he did not feel any pain.
chapterbody 44: One of the celestial doctors wore a white cap, white mask, and white robe, and he stood on the sidelines observing everything. At the end of surgery, this celestial doctor removed his mask.
chapterbody 46: The man then saw his face, and he exclaimed, “It is you, Grand Master Lu!”
chapterbody 48: With this yell, he awoke and realized it was only a dream.
chapterbody 50: This man subsequently went to the hospital for a checkup. The doctor said, “How strange! All your kidney stones have vanished! Your previous ultrasound images showed numerous stones. Now, there is nothing there.”
chapterbody 52: The man hurriedly came to tell me the result of the examination.
chapterbody 57: I said, “Life is but a dream, isn’t it?”
chapterbody 62: They listened to my dharma teaching and found it full of dharma taste.

chapterbody: he Wu family has three children, two boys and one girl, who all had nasal allergies. Their parents did not pay much attention to it initially. However, when their allergies worsened, they realized something was wrong.
chapterbody 3: As soon as they hopped out of bed, their noses started dripping.
chapterbody 5: They would sneeze.
chapterbody 7: Their heads swam.
chapterbody 9: They had trouble concentrating on their homework.
chapterbody 11: They caught colds and came down with fevers and coughs easily. They had bad moods.
chapterbody 13: They went to see medical doctors, but there was not much improvement. They saw traditional Chinese doctors, also without improvement.
chapterbody 15: They had running noses, sneezing, nasal congestion, and trouble breathing on a daily basis….
chapterbody 17: It was a great nuisance to continually wipe their noses with bathroom tissues.
chapterbody 19: Someone suggested they take preventive measures, such as wearing a mask after getting out of bed, keeping the body warm, rinsing the nasal passages, and taking allergy pills.
chapterbody 21: At first those suggestions seemed to help. But after a while, their allergies would flare up again. It got to the point that nothing seemed to help.
chapterbody 23: These allergies became chronic illnesses which gave their parents a huge headache.
chapterbody 25: Someone then told them that Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu would be administering mo-ding after the dharma ceremonies at the Lei Tsang Temple in Caotun, and many people with difficult and complicated illnesses were cured afterwards.
chapterbody 27: The Wu parents were incredulous and said, “Can such things be possible? What kind of power is that?”
chapterbody 29: The messenger said, “It is Buddha power!”
chapterbody 31: The Wu parents still did not believe that a mere laying of hand on the head would cure an illness. Wasn’t that just too outrageous?
chapterbody 33: But, the bearer of the message said, “Why don’t you give it a try? After all, what is there to lose?”
chapterbody 35: So one Saturday, the entire Wu family attended a homa (fire offering) conducted by me at the temple.
chapterbody 37: They found the entire ceremony very majestic.
chapterbody 39: They listened to my dharma teaching and found it full of dharma taste.
chapterbody 41: Finally, they stayed behind to receive the administering of mo-ding. They did not expect to find up to ten thousand people also beseeching mo-ding.
chapterbody 43: When I came upon their family, they called out, “Grand Master Lu, can you touch our noses?”
chapterbody 45: I asked, “What’s wrong with your noses?”
chapterbody 47: They replied, “Nasal allergies!”
chapterbody 49: My hand touched the three children’s noses. My hand vibrated slightly and a hot stream of cosmic energy entered their noses.
chapterbody 51: After tapping three times, I walked away briskly.
chapterbody 53: They called out, “Please touch a few more times!”
chapterbody 54: I turned my head and said, “That should do it!”
chapterbody 56: After the Wu family returned home, the next morning a strange thing happened. The three children’s nasal allergies were gone.
chapterbody 58: They remained symptom free the next day, the next week, and the next month. They were cured!
chapterbody 59: The Wu family commented, “It was indeed very miraculous!”
chapterbody 60: I said, “There are so many efficacious responses. Buddha power is indeed miraculous!”
chapterbody 67: When I heard it, I also found it serendipitous, and an incredible miracle.

chapterbody: student by the name of Ya Jing was applying for a doctoral program at a famous university. He brought his application file to me for a blessing. When my hand touched the documents, I sensed a bright light.
chapterbody 3: I said to Ya Jing, “You will definitely be admitted!”
chapterbody 5: Ya Jing said, “I am just giving it a try. I am not that hopeful because my scores are below the admission standard.”
chapterbody 7: I said, “This is an ironclad admission. I can feel it!” “Really?”
chapterbody 9: Ya Jing asked.
chapterbody 11: “Yes,” I said.
chapterbody 13: Although the applicant himself was skeptical, I told him right off that he would be accepted. I did not just see a bright light, I also saw Guanyin Bodhisattva appearing with a smile, nodding to me.
chapterbody 15: After Ya Jing submitted the application, the school soon notified him that they were rejecting his application because his grades and scores were sub-par.
chapterbody 17: Ya Jing thought to himself, “Grand Master Lu had blessed my application file and he kept saying I would be admitted. But now I am rejected.
chapterbody 19: So evidently Grand Master is sometimes inaccurate too. It looks like one should discount his claims to absolute accuracy in spiritual divination.”
chapterbody 21: He kept his thoughts to himself. But his heart was full of regret. If he had not asked Grand Master Lu for a blessing in the first place, this embarrassing thing would not have happened.
chapterbody 23: How upsetting!
chapterbody 25: He had shared Grand Master Lu’s prediction of his admittance with many other people.
chapterbody 27: Now, how was he going to justify this rejection?
chapterbody 29: Three months later, Ya Jing again received a letter from that university stating the following: One of the students admitted into the doctoral program had come down with a severe illness and was unable to continue. Although Ya Jing did not achieve a certain score in a requisite course, and although his grades did not reach the admittance standard, he nonetheless did well in other subjects. Therefore, the university would admit him to the doctoral program and appoint a professor to tutor him.
chapterbody 31: Heavens!
chapterbody 33: “He was admitted to the program after all!” This was something very incredible.
chapterbody 35: Ya Jing was overjoyed!
chapterbody 37: Ya Jing jumped exuberantly.
chapterbody 39: It was like running up against a stone wall, followed suddenly by a beautiful vista opening up to him. He was admitted.
chapterbody 41: Ya Jing hurried to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple to tell me the news.
chapterbody 43: When I heard it, I also found it serendipitous, and an incredible miracle.
chapterbody 45: However, during the empowerment of mo-ding, I based my predictions on evidence:
chapterbody 47: I saw a bright light, not a swirl of darkness.
chapterbody 49: I saw Guanyin Bodhisattva, not a rakshasa. I saw a smiling face, not a face of tears.
chapterbody 51: Thus I had said, “You will definitely be admitted.”
chapterbody 53: I often pray: “Allow me to truly help people. May my psychic abilities provide reliance for sentient beings.”
chapterbody 56: Reverend Yiming had chanted the Guru’s Heart Mantra over eight million times.
