chapterbody: n olden days, when spiritual cultivators reached a certain level of achievement, at the time of metamorphosis, many auspicious phenomena occurred.
chapterbody 3: 1. The entire body transformed into rainbow lights appearing in the sky.
chapterbody 4: 2. Except for the remains of hair and nails, the rest of the body transformed into rainbow lights.
chapterbody 5: 3. The body would be filled with rolling relics.
chapterbody 7: 5. Relics adhered to the top of the skull.
chapterbody 9: 7. The heart turned into a relic.
chapterbody 10: 8. The tongue turned into a relic.
chapterbody 11: 9. Blood turned into a relic.
chapterbody 16: Among my students, there was an elderly monk who lived in Montreal, Canada.
chapterbody 18: His name was Reverend Yiming and he was in his nineties.
chapterbody 20: In the summer of 2012, Rev. Yiming flew from Montreal to Seattle to see me. It was the last time I saw him. I chatted with him for a little while on the lawn outside True Buddha Quarter.
chapterbody 22: “Rev. Yiming, how are you doing lately?
chapterbody 24: He replied, “Fine! Fine! Fine!”
chapterbody 26: I looked at his face.
chapterbody 28: His complexion was rosy, his brows white, his face rectangular, and when he smiled, he looked like a little child.
chapterbody 30: I asked him, “How is your health?”
chapterbody 32: He replied, “My legs feel a little weak. I lean on a crutch when I walk now. Well, well! I am old!”
chapterbody 34: I gave his shoulder a tap and said, “Reverend, you are a great adept. You will go to the Buddha’s Pure Land in the future.”
chapterbody 36: Reverend Yiming replied, “Grand Master Lu, please escort me there!”
chapterbody 38: I said, “This time I shall escort you. Another time, you will escort me.”
chapterbody 40: Reverend Yiming said, “How can that be?”
chapterbody 42: I replied, “In the future, you will have many relics. I guarantee that sariras will fill your entire body!”
chapterbody 44: I spoke again, “Let’s have a picture taken at the door of the True Buddha Quarter. This is a reunion for the two of us after many lifetimes together.”
chapterbody 46: I knew that:
chapterbody 48: Reverend Yiming had chanted the Guru’s Heart Mantra over eight million times.
chapterbody 50: He had chanted the High King Avalokitesvara Sutra and the True Buddha Sutra several thousand times.
chapterbody 52: He had practiced countless sessions of Guru Yoga, Personal Deity
chapterbody 53: (Yidam) Yoga, and Heruka Yoga.
chapterbody 54: He had attained extraordinary accomplishment in the inner practices.
chapterbody 56: His body, speech, and mind were purified. His wind, channels, and drops were purified.
chapterbody 58: (He had already attained the fruition of arhathood.)
chapterbody 59: Although he was in his nineties, Reverend Yiming had attended every Kalachakra Dharma Ceremony I had conducted.
chapterbody 60: On May 15th, 2013, when I flew to Seattle from Taiwan, I learned that Reverend Yiming had passed away.
chapterbody 66: After the cremation, his entire body turned into sariras, tens of thousands of them, each one the size of a rice grain.
chapterbody 67: During the group practices I have since conducted, Reverend Yiming has appeared seven times to me. In his last appearance, he transformed into an awakened arhat and soared into the sky.
chapterbody 68: Someone asked, “Why was Reverend Yiming able to attain such great accomplishment?”

chaptername: 017 Why Didn’t I See the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds?
chapterbody: female student learned that after another student had chanted the Padmakumara Heart Mantra eight million times, that person had a vision of traveling to the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds.
chapterbody 3: Grand Master Lu has said, “By chanting the Padmakumara Heart Mantra eight million times, one will be reborn to the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds and witness the Pure Land.”
chapterbody 5: So she started chanting the mantra.
chapterbody 7: When she finished chanting seven million times, she did not see anything. When she reached 7.99 million times, she still did not see anything. Finally she indeed reached the eight millionth time, and she almost jumped out of her seat.
chapterbody 9: At the sound of the eight millionth chanting of the mantra, in a flash, before her eyes myriad white clouds appeared emitting brilliant lights.
chapterbody 11: In the middle of the lights a Wish-granting jeweled tree appeared.
chapterbody 13: Padmasambhava sat in the middle of the jeweled tree.
chapterbody 15: Above Padmasambhava’s head were the ancestor gurus.
chapterbody 17: Samantabhadra Tathagata whom she recognized was sitting at the uppermost position.
chapterbody 19: Directly in front of Padmasambhava was Shakyamuni Buddha surrounded by many buddhas of the three times and ten directions.
chapterbody 21: To the right of Padmasambhava were the Three Masters of Reliance - Manjushri, Vajrapani, and Avalokitesvara.
chapterbody 23: To the left of Padmasambhava were Shariputra, Maudgalyayana, and the holy assembly of arhats and pratyekabuddhas.
chapterbody 25: Appearing in the space between the branches and leaves of the Wish-granting jeweled tree were vajra wisdom-kings and dharmapalas. The male vajra wisdom-kings faced outwards while the female ones faced inwards.
chapterbody 27: By her estimation, thousands of buddhas, bodhisattvas, herukas, dharmapalas, dakas, dakinis, and devas appeared in that vision. It was impossible to count them.
chapterbody 29: She came hurriedly to tell me, “What was that I saw?”
chapterbody 31: I replied, “It was the Wish-granting jeweled tree, the Refuge Tree.”
chapterbody 33: She asked, “Why didn’t I see Maha Twin Lotus Ponds?”
chapterbody 35: I replied, “The Wish-granting tree grows in the water of eight virtues. The water of eight virtues is equivalent to the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds.”
chapterbody 37: She said, “I don’t care. It only counts if I can see the eighteen Mahapadmakumaras in the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds!”
chapterbody 39: I laughed, “Do not be so stubborn. You have chanted the heart mantra eight million times and indeed saw thousands of buddhas, bodhisattvas, herukas, dharmapalas, dakas, dakinis, and devas. You should find that satisfactory.”
chapterbody 41: She insisted, “But I wanted to see the eighteen Mahapadmakumaras in the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds! Only that will count.”
chapterbody 43: For a moment, I did not know how to respond.
chapterbody 45: I then said, “Alright! Let me give you a mo-ding. See if anything will happen!”
chapterbody 46: The next day she came back and said, “After the mo-ding, there was nothing. I still did not see the eighteen Mahapadmakumaras in the Maha Twin Lotus Ponds.”
chapterbody 48: At that point, I was also stumped.
chapterbody 50: I then invoked my Yidam to descend.
chapterbody 52: The Yidam told me, “Ask her to chant the True Buddha Sutra three times before coming back for another mo-ding. She will definitely get a vision then!”
chapterbody 55: This time, she ran excitedly up the hill to tell me, “I saw it. I can testify that it is true!”
chapterbody 57: I relayed the words of the Yidam to her.
chapterbody 62: I found it strange that she did not have a vision after the first mo-ding. Only after the second mo-ding was she able to have a vision. Later I learned that she had once taken home three copies of True Buddha Sutra and tossed them casually into the newspaper heap. Because of this, she needed to chant the True Buddha Sutra three times before receiving her vision!
chapterbody 63: Third, chant my heart mantra. If he tries to get close, he will be bounced.

chapterbody: Miss Xu told me a very troubling story.
chapterbody 3: She recently attended the funeral of her good friend’s brother. While riding a motorcycle, this man was hit from behind by a car and flew off the road into the valley below and died.
chapterbody 5: When he was alive, the young man had shown interest in her, and they had gone on outings such as picnics and camping trips together. But, they had not reached the stage of considering each other boyfriend and girlfriend. Each time they met, it was in a group setting with many people around.
chapterbody 7: After the funeral, she went home. It was raining that day, and the house felt cold. It made her gloomy and she thought to herself, “My house is normally very warm. I wonder why it is so chilly today?”
chapterbody 9: The funeral lingered on her mind.
chapterbody 11: The sad eyes in the photo of the deceased at the funeral parlor seemed to follow her.
chapterbody 13: After taking a hot bath, she felt a little more comfortable and went to bed.
chapterbody 15: In the middle of the night, something happened.
chapterbody 17: She woke up suddenly and felt that someone was sleeping next to her. Half-awake, she took a look and was surprised to see her good friend’s brother.
chapterbody 19: His body was light blue.
chapterbody 21: Blood was seeping out of his seven orifices. He spoke to her, “Marry me!”
chapterbody 23: She screamed and woke up completely.
chapterbody 25: She took another look again at the bed yet no one else was there. He had vanished.
chapterbody 27: Then, the following events happened, always in the middle of the night.
chapterbody 29: She saw her good friend’s brother flying above her bed saying, “Marry me!”
chapterbody 31: She turned over in her sleep, and her hand would land on a cold corpse. It was him again, saying, “Marry me!”
chapterbody 33: While asleep, she would feel something pressing down on her. She would push it away, and when the person soared into the air she saw that it was him again, still saying the same words, “Marry me!”
chapterbody 35: These events occurred every night for almost a month.
chapterbody 37: It was so devastating that she felt she was going to have a nervous breakdown!
chapterbody 39: Furthermore:
chapterbody 41: In the middle of the night, the refrigerator door would open on its own.
chapterbody 43: At night someone would knock on the door, and when she opened it, no one was there.
chapterbody 45: The toilet flushed on its own.
chapterbody 47: She heard the sound of showering in the bathroom, but when she looked, no one was there.
chapterbody 49: When she woke in the morning, all the windows would be open.
chapterbody 51: Her slippers disappeared. Someone had tossed them into the garbage can……
chapterbody 53: Miss Xu came to seek me, “Grand Master Lu! What should I do? He is practically living in my home.”
chapterbody 54: I said, “He is not just living in your home. He is following you wherever you go. He is standing next to you right now.”
chapterbody 56: How scary!
chapterbody 58: Miss Xu said, “What should I do?”
chapterbody 59: I said, “This is a very minor thing. First, I will use the Four Vertical-Five Horizontal Seal to empower a sacred boundary around you. He will be unable to get close to you. Second, install the Kalachakra vajra shields in the four corners of your home. He will then be unable to enter your home. Third, chant my heart mantra. If he tries to get close, he will be bounced.”
chapterbody 60: Miss Xu carried my instructions out.
chapterbody 66: Indeed everything became tranquil and normal again.
chapterbody 67: Later, one night, he started following me. I turned around with my hands holding the Transmigration Mudra. His soul body was sucked into the mudra and he was sent through the wheels of transmigration to his next life!

chaptername: 019 A Statue of City God Appearing in the Palm
chapterbody: his is the story of a man who held no religious beliefs. He was a professional soldier who had an impoverished childhood.
chapterbody 3: After enduring much hardship, he applied to military college. After graduation, he led a military life.
chapterbody 5: Military life was simple, and he was a simple person.
chapterbody 7: He had never touched cigarettes, liquor, or gambled.
chapterbody 8: He had never visited places offering sensual pleasures.
chapterbody 9: He also rarely indulged in eating, drinking, or entertainment.
chapterbody 11: He enjoyed reading, learning, and quietude. He went to the movies occasionally.
chapterbody 13: During his military training, he had developed a very strong willpower. He preferred to lead a disciplinary life with unblemished habits.
chapterbody 15: To him, living a life of peace and few desires was no different from living the life of a Buddhist who chanted sutras and strictly followed Buddhist precepts.
chapterbody 17: Hence, he did not believe in anything!
chapterbody 19: His friends tried to persuade him to become a Buddhist, but he was unmoved.
chapterbody 21: Every day, he went to work without demanding any luxury from life.
chapterbody 23: One day, after a bowel movement, he saw fresh blood in the toilet. He was terrified. This happened continuously for several weeks.
chapterbody 25: He went to the drug store to purchase some over-the-counter medicine for intestinal inflammation. It did not help.
chapterbody 27: He went to a clinic to see a doctor and was prescribed an antibiotic. It did not help.
chapterbody 29: Finally, a colonoscopy revealed colon cancer. The attending physician advocated surgical resection.
chapterbody 31: He was worried there would be complications after resection of the bowel.
chapterbody 33: Someone told him about Grand Master Sheng-yen Lu at the Lei Tsang Temple at Tiger Hill in Caotun. Many miracles happened to people after receiving mo-ding from the Grand Master.
chapterbody 35: He scoffed, "This is a bright world, yet so many people spread fallacies to deceive others!"
chapterbody 37: When his acquaintance urged him to give mo-ding a try, he said, "Many people have criticized Sheng-yen Lu, and rumors surround him. I am not interested in going to see him."
chapterbody 39: Failing to persuade him, his friend left but decided to give him a book, Seventy Spiritual Dreams, written by Grand Master Sheng-yen Lu.
chapterbody 41: He started reading the book and found the stories quite refreshing.
chapterbody 43: Later, his friend came back to persuade him to visit me. Finding it difficult to decline his friend’s kind intentions, and because the trip did not cost much, he decided to give the suggestion a try.
chapterbody 45: When I placed my hand on his head, I saw a statue of the city god, Cheng Huang, appearing amidst light in my palm.
chapterbody 47: I said to him, "The city god Cheng Huang has come to help you!" (Cheng Huang is the local earth deity.)
chapterbody 49: He replied, "Nonsense. I don’t believe in any religion. Where did this Cheng Huang come from?"
chapterbody 51: "Have you ever made offerings to him?"
chapterbody 53: "Of course not!" he replied.
chapterbody 54: I asked him, "Are you sure there is no connection? Try to remember, give this some thought. There must be some connection."
chapterbody 56: This officer suddenly jumped up and said, "I remember now. When I was small, the Cheng Huang Temple was next door to our house. I often went to play in the temple and emulated the adults by joining my palms and nodding my head to him. On one occasion my parents installed an annual lamp at the City God Temple for me."
chapterbody 58: Oh heavens!
chapterbody 59: This was remarkably accurate!
chapterbody 60: In his entire life, the City God was the only deity he had ever joined palms to pay homage to.
chapterbody 66: After receiving mo-ding from me, his cancer miraculously started shrinking and eventually vanished. The attending physician wanted to perform surgery, but could find nothing to operate on.
chapterbody 67: He came to thank me and took refuge in me. I said, "Go thank Cheng Huang!"
chapterbody 68: He replied, "The Cheng Huang Temple is in Penghu. I will indeed go and thank him when I am free!"

chapterbody: n May 22, 2013, I was at the Ling Shen Ching Tze Temple in Redmond.
chapterbody 3: Early morning on the 22nd, while sleeping, I dreamt of a man with a pair of white wings, someone who would be referred to as an angel in Catholicism or Christianity.
chapterbody 5: This angel said to me, “I am the angel Gabriel.”
chapterbody 7: I asked, “Is there anything you need?”
chapterbody 9: The angel said, “You will know when the time comes.”
chapterbody 11: In this dream state, all I heard was the name of the angel Gabriel, and I was unaware of anything else.
chapterbody 13: The evening of the 22nd, after finishing dinner, an old man walked up to me with a walking staff and asked me for a blessing. He handed me a slip of paper.
chapterbody 15: The paper read: “I have fallen more than ten times this week. A scan found a big blood clot in my brain. The doctors have said that this blood clot needs to be removed in order to cure my falling. Grand Master Lu, please give me a blessing.”
chapterbody 17: I immediately extended my hand and placed it on his head.
chapterbody 19: I focused my mind on visualizing my Yidam Golden Mother of Jade Pond to descend. But, Golden Mother did not arrive and instead the angel Gabriel appeared. The angel Gabriel wanted to help this old man with a walking staff.
chapterbody 21: A flow of cosmic energy streamed through my palm continuously into the old man’s head.
chapterbody 23: I spoke, “A miracle will happen! You will be well!”
chapterbody 25: On May 23rd, this old man went to the hospital to repeat the scan. After the first scan, the blood clot was not seen. The doctors did a second scan, and they still could not see any clot.
chapterbody 27: They reviewed the previous scan for comparison. Last time, there was a large blood clot on the scan. But these two new scans showed nothing.
chapterbody 29: The doctor called out, “How odd!”
chapterbody 31: Another doctor said, “This is inconceivable!”
chapterbody 33: The old man called to say that he would come in a few days to thank me.
chapterbody 35: Several days later when he arrived, I asked curiously, “Who is the angel Gabriel?”
chapterbody 37: The old man replied, “I don’t know.”
chapterbody 39: I asked, “Do you have a statue of an angel at your home?”
chapterbody 41: The old man thought for a while and said, “My son went one time to Syria on a business trip and saw a wooden sculpture of an angel in an antique store. He found it very beautiful and purchased it. After he returned, he casually placed it somewhere in the house. I saw that it was an angel with wings and felt that it must be something sacred. I placed it in the uppermost shelf inside a cabinet to show my respect. I did not know if it was a Catholic or Protestant angel.”
chapterbody 43: Finally I went to look up information on the angel Gabriel myself. The information I found was amazing!
chapterbody 45: According to Islam, when Mohammad was forty years old, while on retreat in a mountain cave, the Cave of Hira, three miles from Mecca, an angel had appeared to him confirming him as a prophet from the true lord Allah and revealed that the truth he was to spread was contained in the Quran.
chapterbody 47: The name of this angel of revelation was: Gabriel!
chapterbody 51: The following night, Guanyin Bodhisattva again spoke to her, “Ask Grand Master Lu to stroke your belly!”
chapterbody 53: Sheng-yen Lu

chaptername: 021 Ask Grand Master Lu to Stroke Your Belly
chapterbody: young pregnant woman was suffering from nausea and vomiting. She was so uncomfortable that she felt that the fetus inside would fall out! She even had occasional spotting.
chapterbody 3: She went to see the doctor.
chapterbody 5: The doctor said, “There is a chance you may have a miscarriage. You should stay in bed to keep the baby.”
chapterbody 7: So, following the doctor’s instruction, she did less walking and spent most of her time lying in bed.
chapterbody 9: This young woman was a Buddhist. A nun from the Puji Temple visited her and told her:
chapterbody 11: 1. Chant the Great Compassion Dharani and drink the Great Compassion Dharani Water.
chapterbody 13: 2. Chant the “Universal Gate” chapter from the Lotus Sutra as it helps women in slow and difficult deliveries.
chapterbody 15: 3. Chant the Medicine Sutra as it can cure illnesses.
chapterbody 17: The woman chanted the Great Compassion Dharani, the “Universal Gate” chapter, and the Medicine Sutra as instructed by the nun.
chapterbody 19: The result turned out well. After two months of bed rest, the fetus stabilized and she no longer felt it would drop out. She also felt much more comfortable.
chapterbody 21: She went to the Puji Temple to make offerings to the sangha team.
chapterbody 22: She attended dharma teachings.
chapterbody 23: She sprouted bodhicitta and registered as a sponsor to the temple.
chapterbody 25: A nun explained to her how the Buddhadharma is most effective in curing diseases. She was joyous to hear it.
chapterbody 27: Around the time of her seventh and eighth month of pregnancy, during a routine checkup, the doctor discovered that the baby was in breech presentation. Before delivery, a baby’s head should face down and feet up, but in her case, the feet were down and the head was up.
chapterbody 29: The doctor gave her some pictures illustrating the kind of exercises she had to do to remedy the situation. The exercises would cause the baby to slowly turn around, so that its presentation would be head-first at birth.
chapterbody 31: The young woman worked very hard on the exercises.
chapterbody 33: As the delivery date got closer, the obstetrician did an ultrasound scan and discovered that the baby had moved into a head-first position. How wonderful.
chapterbody 35: But, when the doctor placed his stethoscope on her abdomen, he found that he could not hear any heart beats.
chapterbody 37: He carefully inspected her, and indeed there was no heartbeat. The baby had died!
chapterbody 39: The doctor sighed.
chapterbody 41: The young woman turned ashen.
chapterbody 43: The doctor told her there was nothing else to do but to remove the dead fetus surgically.
chapterbody 45: After the young woman went home, that night she tossed and turned, unable to fall asleep. She was very sad.
chapterbody 47: Finally as she was about to fall asleep, she saw Guanyin walk out of the Guanyin picture hanging on her wall.
chapterbody 49: Guanyin spoke to her, “Ask Grand Master Lu to stroke your belly!”
chapterbody 51: After she finished speaking, Guanyin walked back into the picture. When this happened the first time, she considered it a dream that reflected her mind state during the day.
chapterbody 53: The following night, Guanyin Bodhisattva again spoke to her, “Ask Grand Master Lu to stroke your belly!”
chapterbody 54: This time, she asked around, “Who is Grand Master Lu?”
chapterbody 56: Someone told her that Grand Master Lu was delivering dharma discourses at the Lei Tsang Temple. So she indeed came and stood in queue among the crowds.
chapterbody 58: When I walked by, she lifted her blouse to show me her belly and said, “Grand Master Lu, please stroke the baby in my belly! The baby’s heart has stopped beating.”
chapterbody 59: I was startled, and I tapped her belly lightly.
chapterbody 60: Just like that, the baby’s heart started beating again. Later the woman gave birth to a fair, chubby, healthy boy.
chapterbody 66: This young woman brought many people to take refuge in me!
chapterbody 68: Inside these lights, I saw another Bai Wuchang wearing the same headpiece (Lucky to See Me).

chapterbody: he night market, as usual, was jam-packed with throngs of men and women. There were many street vendors, mostly selling food items and clothes.
chapterbody 3: They were doing brisk business. They reminded me of the folk “Prayer for Shrewd Business.”
chapterbody 5: When I leave my house,
chapterbody 7: To the east a horde of buddhas,
chapterbody 8: To the west a horde of buddhas,
chapterbody 9: To the south a horde of buddhas,
chapterbody 10: To the north a horde of buddhas.
chapterbody 11: All my merchandise - sold!
chapterbody 13: I started chanting it and thought to myself what a wonderful and appropriate prayer for these night market vendors to chant.
chapterbody 15: At that time, I noticed a stranger paying special attention to me, so I turned to glance at him.
chapterbody 17: This stranger was walking around the night market. He was of medium height but had a powerful build. His face was square and dark.
chapterbody 19: When I took a look behind him, I was shocked:
chapterbody 21: Standing behind him was a very tall spiritual guard, the Seventh Master Bai Wuchang, wearing a very tall hat with the words “Lucky to See Me” written upon it.
chapterbody 23: The invisible “Bai Wuchang,” known as the Seventh Master, was a lieutenant of the City God Cheng Huang in charge of bringing the spirits of the dead to the underworld.
chapterbody 25: I was surprised to see him!
chapterbody 27: The stranger said to me, “Are you Grand Master Sheng-yen Lu?” I replied, “Yes, I am.”
chapterbody 29: The stranger said, “A long time ago, I started reading your books. I have also watched your dharma talks on television. That is why I recognized you.”
chapterbody 31: I said, “Thank you!”
chapterbody 33: The stranger said, “I have heard that your spiritual divination is extremely accurate. Do you think you can give me a reading?”
chapterbody 35: I asked, “What kind of reading?”
chapterbody 37: He replied, “Tell me about my profession!”
chapterbody 39: So right there in the middle of the street, I extended my hand to observe the light on my palm. Inside these lights, I saw another Bai Wuchang wearing the same headpiece (Lucky to See Me).
chapterbody 41: Strange! Strange!
chapterbody 43: Why was the Seventh Master Bai Wuchang there again? I was confused. What relationship was there between his profession and the Seventh Master Bai Wuchang?
chapterbody 45: The stranger laughed, “It is not easy to read my profession, is it?” I was embarrassed. Apart from seeing Bai Wuchang, I could not see anything else. Could it be that this person was going to be recalled by Bai Wuchang and lose his life soon? So therefore, there was no need to figure out his profession?
chapterbody 47: The stranger said, “You cannot divine my profession. Ha ha!”
chapterbody 49: I said, “To be frank, I really cannot figure it out. However, in my palm light, I see a white colored Seventh Master Bai Wuchang. What connection is there between you and him?
chapterbody 51: This time, it was the stranger’s turn to be shocked. He knelt down and asked to take refuge.
chapterbody 53: “What is it?”
chapterbody 54: The stranger replied, “Grand Master Lu, to be honest with you, I have been to many fortune tellers and not one was able to divine my profession, which is this: I am the carrier of the icon Bai Wuchang during religious processions.”
chapterbody 58: After this dream, he followed my instructions to formally take refuge in Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu.
chapterbody 59: Sheng-yen Lu

chaptername: 023 Who Can Bestow Me an Empowerment in a Dream?
chapterbody: young man who had deep faith in Vajrayana practices wanted a Tibetan teacher to be his Vajra Root Guru to give him initiations and empowerments. He had read many of my books, yet he did not take refuge in me.
chapterbody 3: Why did he not want to take refuge in Grand Master Lu? His reasons were:
chapterbody 5: 1. Grand Master Lu is Chinese and from Taiwan.
chapterbody 7: 3. Too many rumors surround him.
chapterbody 8: 4. It is hard to tell if the Vajrayana dharma taught by him is real.
chapterbody 9: 5. True Buddha School may very well be a False Buddha School.
chapterbody 11: This young man greatly admired and wanted very much to take refuge in Chatral Rinpoche, an eminent elder in the Nyingma and Kagyu traditions.
chapterbody 13: Chatral Rinpoche is over one hundred years old!
chapterbody 15: One day, this young man read in the news that the wife of Chatral
chapterbody 16: Rinpoche, Kamala, would be visiting the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple. So he went to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple.
chapterbody 18: When he arrived, he was astonished by what he saw.
chapterbody 20: Twenty to thirty thousand people from around the world had converged at the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple. Kamala sat in the first row of the VIP section, and she repeatedly nodded as she listened to Grand Master Lu’s dharma teaching.
chapterbody 22: He pushed through the crowd to try to speak to Kamala.
chapterbody 24: But, the eminent Buddha Mother Kamala had a security guard with her.
chapterbody 26: So, he asked from afar, “Is Grand Master Lu a living buddha?”
chapterbody 28: Kamala replied, “Yes.”
chapterbody 30: He asked, “What does Chatral Rinpoche think of Grand Master Lu?”
chapterbody 32: Kamala replied, “He is a young hero.”
chapterbody 34: He wanted to ask a third question, but someone interrupted him. That day, not caring one way or the other he received the wealth deity Manohara Vasudhara Empowerment. He had only come to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple out of admiration for Buddha Mother Kamala.
chapterbody 36: This young man then heard others saying that the administering of mo-ding by Grand Master Lu that evening would be even more remarkable and was often accompanied by miraculous happenings.
chapterbody 38: So he decided to stay behind and wait for Grand Master Lu’s mo-ding.
chapterbody 40: He thought to himself, “I have longed for an empowerment of Guru Dragpo, the wrathful form of Guru Rinpoche. But after attending many Tibetan Buddhist centers, I have not found a lama giving this empowerment. When Grand Master Lu gives me mo-ding, I will pray to him to give me this empowerment in my dream. If nothing happens, then this Grand Master Lu is a fraud. If something happens, then I will see!”
chapterbody 42: He queued and received the mo-ding.
chapterbody 44: That night, in his dream, Grand Master Lu indeed appeared. Grand Master Lu said, “I know you wish to receive the Guru
chapterbody 46: Dragpo Empowerment.”
chapterbody 48: The young man asked, “Then why don’t you give me the empowerment?”
chapterbody 50: I said, “Empowerments must be given in the proper sequence. You have not even taken refuge, how can I give you this empowerment?”
chapterbody 52: After this dream, he followed my instructions to formally take refuge in Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu.
chapterbody 55: This young man was indeed very diligent in his practice.
chapterbody 57: After receiving the Refuge empowerment, Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu gave this young man a formal empowerment of Guru Dragpo.
chapterbody 62: He has experienced the early stages of yogic response already. He said, “Everything about Grand Master Lu is real!”
chapterbody 63: During ordinary times, chant Namo Amituofo anywhere, anytime, with or without a counter.

chapterbody: n elderly Buddhist layman felt that he was getting on in years. After practicing the Tantrayana inner fire for several years, he still did not feel any different. The lower half of his body still felt weak and cold.
chapterbody 2: With the removal of my karmic defilement and the thirty-two times of mo-ding, I shall develop thirty-two transformation bodies.
chapterbody 3: He practiced the “drop method,” but drops did not descend. He practiced the “elevation of drops,” but could not feel any drops arising.
chapterbody 5: He tried to bring qi into his central channel but he could only hold his breath for ten seconds, not close to half a minute, nor a minute. So, he also had no success with the Baopingqi (Treasure Vase Qi) practice.
chapterbody 7: He felt very discouraged.
chapterbody 9: One time, after I gave him mo-ding, he had a dream.
chapterbody 11: Grand Master Lu spoke to him, “Just chant honestly!”
chapterbody 13: The elderly layman came to me, “Grand Master Lu, I dreamt of you, and you told me to just do my chanting honestly! Please tell me, how does one chant honestly?”
chapterbody 15: I replied:
chapterbody 17: At your shrine, enshrine Amitabha in the middle, Guanyin Bodhisattva (Avalokitesvara) to the right, and Dashizhi Bodhisattva (Mahasthamaprapta) to the left.
chapterbody 19: Set up either five or eight offerings.
chapterbody 21: The five offerings are flowers, incense, lamp, tea, and fruits.
chapterbody 23: The eight offerings are flowers, incense, lamp, tea, fruits, pure water, bathing water, and a conch.
chapterbody 25: Twice a day, in the morning and evening, prior to chanting, wash your hands, rinse your mouth, light incense at the shrine, make an offering of tea, join your palms in homage, and chant the following:
chapterbody 27: With one mind, I prostrate to His Holiness Namo Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu. (Three times, each accompanied by a prostration.)
chapterbody 29: With one mind, I prostrate to Namo the Fundamental Teacher Shakyamuni Buddha. (Three times, each accompanied by a prostration.)
chapterbody 31: With one mind, I prostrate to Namo Amitabha Buddha to the West. (Three times, each accompanied by a prostration.)
chapterbody 33: With one mind, I prostrate to Namo Guanyin Bodhisattva. (Three times, each accompanied by a prostration.)
chapterbody 35: With one mind, I prostrate to Namo Dashizhi Bodhisattva. (Three times, each accompanied by a prostration.)
chapterbody 37: With one mind, I prostrate to Namo Pure Ocean Assembly of all bodhisattvas. (Three times, each accompanied by a prostration.)
chapterbody 39: Then make the dedication:
chapterbody 41: May all who uphold the name of Amitabha Buddha
chapterbody 43: Be born together in the Pure Land of His Western Paradise Repaying the Fourfold Generosity from above
chapterbody 45: And aiding those who suffer in the Three Paths below Upon seeing the Buddha
chapterbody 47: May I be liberated from the cycle of birth and death
chapterbody 49: And may I develop the qualities of Buddhahood And thus free all who suffer.
chapterbody 51: (If one wants to chant mantras, one may also chant Om, ah-mee-deh-wah, seh, Om, mani padme hum, and Om xi xi, suo, so-ha.
chapterbody 53: Do this homework twice a day, morning and evening.
chapterbody 54: During ordinary times, chant Namo Amituofo anywhere, anytime, with or without a counter.
chapterbody 56: The elderly lay Buddhist asked me, “May I chant the Amitabha Sutra?”
chapterbody 58: I said, “Of course.”
chapterbody 59: He asked, “May I chant the High King Avalokitesvara Sutra?”
chapterbody 66: He asked, “May I chant the True Buddha Sutra?”
chapterbody 68: He asked, “And the Rebirth Mantra too?”

chaptername: 025 Guanyin Bodhisattva Asked Me to Come
chapterbody: ne time during mo-ding, among the hordes of people, I discovered the presence of a non-human.
chapterbody 3: People were jostling and extending their heads for me to give them mo-ding.
chapterbody 5: And, an invisible flying object was moving swiftly up and down, crisscrossing above the human heads.
chapterbody 7: Although I was aware of this, I did not say anything. I extended my arm to give him mo-ding in the air. He laughed happily.
chapterbody 9: I also laughed!
chapterbody 11: This “non-human” was very eccentric, darting here and there like a dragonfly. Every so often, he would extend his head and ask me to give him mo-ding. He received mo-ding thirty-two times altogether.
chapterbody 13: I asked, “Who are you?”
chapterbody 15: He replied, “I am a genie.”
chapterbody 17: I asked, “Why have you come for the mo-ding?”
chapterbody 19: He replied, “Guanyin Bodhisattva has asked me to come.”
chapterbody 21: I asked, “Was there any special reason that Guanyin Bodhisattva asked you to come and receive mo-ding thirty-two times?”
chapterbody 23: He replied, “Through mo-ding and the empowerment of the Buddha power, I will be able to eradicate some karmic defilement to purify myself. Guanyin Bodhisattva took pity on me. With the removal of my karmic defilement and the thirty-two times of mo-ding, I shall develop thirty-two transformation bodies.”
chapterbody 25: “Like the thirty-two emanations of Guanyin Bodhisattva?”
chapterbody 27: “Yes.”
chapterbody 29: “To do what with?”
chapterbody 31: “In the south there is a small temple newly built by the villagers. The main deity is Guanyin Bodhisattva. Guanyin Bodhisattva asked me to live and settle there instead of roaming around.”
chapterbody 33: I snapped, “Little genie, what merits do you have that would entitle you to residence in the temple and the enjoyment of offerings from humans? And you also make this claim by the name of Guanyin Bodhisattva?”
chapterbody 35: He replied, “Although I am a genie, I have never harmed anyone. I actually have been helping people silently. Guanyin Bodhisattva had mercy on me and believes that I am amenable. That was why she asked me to receive the thirty-two mo-dings from you, so I can manifest as the thirty-two Guanyin emanations to help more people.”
chapterbody 37: I was touched.
chapterbody 39: Although he is a genie, he knows not to harm even one sentient being.
chapterbody 41: Additionally, he tries his best to help all sentient beings. The former is the practice of Hinayana.
chapterbody 43: The latter is the practice of Mahayana.
chapterbody 45: A genie can also cultivate to become a bodhisattva! It is indeed rare!
chapterbody 47: I asked him, “What is the name of your future temple?”
chapterbody 49: He replied, “XX Temple.”
chapterbody 51: Some time later, I traveled south to a rural area near Kaohsiung, and a bodhisattva appeared to tell me, “XX Temple is coming up!
chapterbody 53: I looked outside the car window and saw a temple at the foot of the mountain. It was indeed XX Temple. It was a spectacular temple with red walls and green tiles.
chapterbody 54: I asked in astonishment, “Is that your temple?”
chapterbody 56: He answered, “The small temple has become a big temple now!”
chapterbody 59: I extended and pressed my hand on their heads, and I suddenly felt something quite different.

chapterbody: retired couple, students of mine, love to travel. After they return from trips, they always attend our Saturday group cultivations and other dharma ceremonies. They love to join seven day cruises, stay up late and enjoy cruise activities freely.
chapterbody 3: They have been on Alaskan cruises, Caribbean cruises, European cruises, Southeast Asia cruises, and Northeast Asia cruises. They have joined cruises all around the world.
chapterbody 5: They have told me that cruises are the best ways to travel the world as the entire ship is like a giant waterpark.
chapterbody 7: If you love eating, the ship has restaurants offering gourmet foods from around the world.
chapterbody 9: If you love drinking, there are bars and cafes.
chapterbody 11: If you love entertainment, there are night clubs, movie theaters, karaoke singing, swimming pools, spas, casinos, etc.
chapterbody 13: If you enjoy physical activities and entertainment, there are yoga classes, dancing clubs, exercise rooms, mahjong tables, etc.
chapterbody 15: Going on a cruise is equivalent to taking a retreat from land to visit paradise at sea. It helps you forget your problems.
chapterbody 17: When a large cruise ship arrives at a city, it docks to allow tourists on board an opportunity to take excursions. In the evening, when everyone returns, the ship continues to the next stop.
chapterbody 19: This couple had just returned from the Southern Pacific Islands, and they were about to fly to Orlando, Florida to take a cruise to the Bahamas.
chapterbody 21: When I heard their plan, I was very envious.
chapterbody 23: Before their departure, the couple had asked me to give them a mo-ding blessing for a safe trip.
chapterbody 25: I extended and pressed my hand on their heads, and I suddenly felt something quite different. My entire body grew hot like it had caught fire.
chapterbody 27: This mo-ding blessing lasted a little longer than usual, and when I took a closer look at my palm I saw “a fire burning!”
chapterbody 29: This time, finding myself unable to say “Have a good trip,” I said instead, “Something seems to be not quite right!”
chapterbody 31: They were also surprised, “What is not quite right?
chapterbody 33: I replied, “Fire!”
chapterbody 35: The couple asked, “Should we be concerned?”
chapterbody 37: I replied, “I just saw a fire and nothing else. That usually isn’t a concern.”
chapterbody 39: (I spoke honestly.)
chapterbody 41: The couple laughed, “We are taking a trip on a large cruise ship. The captain is a good friend of ours. There shouldn’t be any problems. This ship is the largest cruise ship in the world now.”
chapterbody 43: I gave them my blessing.
chapterbody 45: I had indeed not expected the largest cruise ship in the world, while sailing the Caribbean Sea, to have a fire in its engine which completely engulfed and burnt down its large restaurant and dining room area.
chapterbody 47: All the passengers assembled on deck and prepared to flee on lifeboats. It was a chaotic and terrifying scene with three thousand people scrambling to exit the ship.
chapterbody 49: In the end, everyone was rescued. It was indeed a merciful thing!

chapterbody: n old man loved his grandchild dearly. The little boy was named Youyou.
chapterbody 3: Youyou was born after his father passed away. Because his mother worked, his grandfather helped take care of him, and a deep bond developed between grandfather and grandson.
chapterbody 5: When Youyou was small, his grandfather took meticulous care of him. He fed him, bathed him, and put him to bed.
chapterbody 7: On holidays, if his mother did not visit, grandpa would take Youyou to the zoo, miniature world, aquarium, and other theme parks.
chapterbody 9: Grandpa treated Youyou as if he were his own son.
chapterbody 11: Later, when Youyou started school, the first thing he asked when he came home from school was, “Ah Gong, where are you?”
chapterbody 13: Youyou was a clever boy.
chapterbody 15: He knew how to make tea for Ah Gong. He washed dishes.
chapterbody 17: He took out the trash.
chapterbody 19: He gave Ah Gong massages.
chapterbody 21: He also told funny stories about school so that Ah Gong could not stop smiling.
chapterbody 23: Ah Gong loved this grandson to death. The two of them even played hide and seek.
chapterbody 25: When Youyou was nine years old, he caught influenza. Ah Gong looked after him carefully. But, unfortunately, Youyou developed complications and died.
chapterbody 27: With Youyou passing away, the old man was heartbroken.
chapterbody 29: Ah Gong could only scream to the sky, “Heaven! Why are you so ruthless! Why are you so cruel!”
chapterbody 31: Oh! What was there to do?
chapterbody 33: Later someone introduced this grandfather to the True Buddha School. He registered Youyou as a main supplicant for five consecutive times during the True Buddha School bardo deliverance ceremonies.
chapterbody 35: Grand Master Lu gave him a reply, “Amitabha has guided your grandson Youyou to rebirth in the Buddha’s Pure Land.”
chapterbody 37: This grandfather sought out a medium, and the medium said, “Youyou is in hell, locked up in a city for those who have died in vain. He is suffering from cold and starvation there.”
chapterbody 39: When the grandfather heard this, he became so angry that he stopped coming to the Lei Tsang Temple. He believed the medium and burnt case after case of joss papers to Youyou.
chapterbody 41: The medium said, “Youyou wants food!”
chapterbody 43: Grandpa sent Youyou’s favorite foods to the medium, and the latter ate them all.
chapterbody 45: The medium said, “Youyou wants money!” Grandpa gave the medium a lot of money.
chapterbody 47: The medium said, “Youyou is about to be reborn, he needs a transit fee!”
chapterbody 49: Grandpa again brought money to the medium.
chapterbody 51: Later, his friends saw that this was getting out of hand and they advised the old man not to be fooled by the medium anymore. They said, “Why don’t you go up the mountain to get a mo-ding from Grand Master Lu? Perhaps Youyou might appear in your dream.”
chapterbody 53: After giving it some thought, the old man decided to give it a try. After all, since Youyou’s passing three years ago, he had not appeared even once in grandpa’s dream.
chapterbody 54: The old man came to the Lei Tsang Temple and asked me to give him a mo-ding.
chapterbody 56: That same night, he had a dream:
chapterbody 58: Youyou was riding on a lotus, and he was surrounded by many other lotus youths, the Padmakumaras.
chapterbody 59: Youyou said to the old man, “Ah Gong! Do not worry! I was reborn to the Buddha’s Pure Land a long time ago!”
chapterbody 66: I noticed the lights in my palm shooting up to the sky, and I said, “This person will get well!”

chaptername: 028 Extending an Arm from the Clouds
chapterbody: woman was staying at a bed and breakfast in a rural area. That night, the moon was bright and the air very fresh. The woman decided to take a walk outside along the countryside trail.
chapterbody 2: When I come upon various gods, I always join my palms to salute them as a sign of respect.
chapterbody 3: From afar, she saw the vague shadows of a few young girls. The occasional sounds of their laughter reached her, and she then saw, very clearly, one of the girls waving at her.
chapterbody 5: So she quickened her steps.
chapterbody 7: But, when she got closer, a strange thing happened. The young girls disappeared.
chapterbody 9: The sound of their laughter also had vanished.
chapterbody 11: She looked around. Except for a small hill overgrown with weeds ahead, there was nothing else.
chapterbody 13: Under the moonlight, there was no other soul. Where did those young girls go?
chapterbody 15: Where did their sounds of laughter and talking come from? Who had waved to her?
chapterbody 17: What she had experienced was absolutely not an illusion. She mustered up the courage to brush aside the wild grass as tall as a man.
chapterbody 19: A shiver went up her spine. In front of her were several isolated graves.
chapterbody 21: She shuddered, and as if possessed, her body twisted and jiggled as she walked back to the bed and breakfast.
chapterbody 23: That night, she became ill with chills and fevers. She notified her family to come pick her up at the bed and breakfast. After she was brought home, she started talking incoherently and her life turned topsy-turvy.
chapterbody 25: She had frequent nightmares.
chapterbody 27: She spoke weird words. She was coming apart at the seams.
chapterbody 29: She became abnormal, crying, laughing, and threatening to kill herself.
chapterbody 31: She once had a sunny disposition, but after her stay at the bed and breakfast, she became mentally ill.
chapterbody 33: Doctors said, “She has schizophrenia!”
chapterbody 35: Mediums said, “She is possessed by spirits!”
chapterbody 37: But, even with oral medications and injections, the state of her health fluctuated. When mediums performed exorcisms, she sometimes got better, and sometimes got worse.
chapterbody 39: When this woman’s paternal aunt, a student of True Buddha School, found out about this, she wanted to bring her to the temple to consult Grand Master Lu for a resolution.
chapterbody 41: But the woman’s family refused. Her family believed in a master of another religious group. They sought healing from this master, but after a period of time, there was no improvement.
chapterbody 43: Finally the aunt brought a photograph of the woman to Lei Tsang Temple and entreated me to lay my hand on her picture.
chapterbody 45: Her aunt said, “Please lay your hand on her picture a little longer!” I indeed placed my hand on her picture a little longer. I noticed the lights in my palm shooting up to the sky, and I said, “This person will get well!”
chapterbody 47: After the laying of hands on her photograph, the woman had a dream:
chapterbody 48: She saw a Padmakumara standing atop a lotus in the sky. The Padmakumara extended an arm down from the clouds into her brain and pulled out four to five small bugs.
chapterbody 50: After the dream, her mental health improved day by day.
chapterbody 52: Her aunt asked her, “How do you know it was Padmakumara who had saved you?”
chapterbody 55: I said to them, “It wasn’t me. The buddhas and bodhisattvas saved you.”
chapterbody 57: She replied, “The Padmakumara said he was Grand Master Lu.” This time, she and her family came to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple to thank me.

chapterbody: ne evening, after finishing dinner at the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple, I prepared to give mo-ding to all the devotees already filling the temple plaza.
chapterbody 3: But when I walked outside, I was shocked by what I saw.
chapterbody 5: There were not only crowds of people waiting, but there were also multiple deities in the empty space.
chapterbody 7: “What is happening?” I wondered.
chapterbody 9: When I looked more closely, I saw Jade Emperor, the supreme deity, as well!
chapterbody 11: Yuanshi Tianzun, the supreme being of Taoism, had also arrived.
chapterbody 13: The lords of the three realms were present: heavenly official Rao, earth official Shun, and water official Yu.
chapterbody 15: The Super-emperor God Xuantian was there, one hand holding a sword, the other holding his index finger upright.
chapterbody 17: Master Lu Zu, also known as Lu Dongbin, one of the Eight Immortals, also had descended.
chapterbody 19: Baosheng Dadi, a god of medicine, was there.
chapterbody 21: The Heavenly Holy Mother Lin Moniang, Mazu, was there.
chapterbody 23: Lord Guang, the patron saint of warriors, was there.
chapterbody 25: Jiutian Xuannu, the deva of ninth heaven, was there.
chapterbody 27: The deity Chen Jingu, also known as Madam Linshui, was there.
chapterbody 29: The Third Prince Nezha, was there.
chapterbody 31: City Gods, regional gods, local earth gods, etc.…
chapterbody 33: Oh heavens! Why were so many deities here? They all descended upon the empty space above the plaza at Lei Tsang Temple.
chapterbody 35: I was relayed the following account from the owner of a Taoist temple, who is a student of Grand Master Lu. He had a dream in which the deities at his temple had decided to travel to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple to seek a purification mo-ding from Grand Master.
chapterbody 37: Therefore, each devotee of the temple was asked to carry a statue of a deity to the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple and invite Grand Master Lu to give them each mo-ding as a cleansing.
chapterbody 39: I thought to myself, “How can this be? These gods are typically the objects of worship and kowtow. How can I give them mo-ding?”
chapterbody 41: The deities said, “It is permissible.”
chapterbody 43: Golden Mother of Jade Pond said, “It is permissible.”
chapterbody 45: I said, “This cannot be happening.”
chapterbody 47: Although I am a diligent cultivator who performs Eye Opening Ceremonies for deities from buddhas above to earth gods below, the ritual of consecration is after all not the same as the ritual of mo-ding.
chapterbody 49: Didn’t it seem a bit self-aggrandizing for me to bestow mo-ding to these deities?
chapterbody 51: When I come upon various gods, I always join my palms to salute them as a sign of respect. What reason is there now for me to pat their heads?
chapterbody 53: The owner of the temple said, “That was what my dream had instructed me to do. Please raise your hand to comply with our wish.” I figured out a solution. During the process of mo-ding, when I came upon someone carrying a statue, to signify my respect, I would similarly hold up the statue with both hands and touch the head of the deity with my head.
chapterbody 54: Hence, during many occasions of mo-ding, you can see Grand Master extending his hand to give mo-ding to the students; and for those who held the statues of deities, Grand Master Lu would lower his head and touch his forehead upon the forehead of the gods to signify respect.
chapterbody 56: I am a Buddhist.
chapterbody 58: I am a vajra acharya, the founder of True Buddha School.
chapterbody 59: But, I respect all religions, sects, buddhas, bodhisattvas, herukas, dharma protectors, dakas and dakinis, devas, celestials living in space, celestials living on earth, as well as all the beings in the six realms of existence.
chapterbody 66: Yet now my understanding of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma becomes more and more lucid.

chapterbody: y personal study of Tantrayana began with an empowerment from Reverend Liaoming. Later, Reverend Liaoming told me that he belonged to the Nyingma School (of Tibetan Buddhism) under Norlha Rinpoche.
chapterbody 2: Hinayana, Mahayana, Yoga Tantra, and Highest Yoga Tantra. Personally, I find the following figures to be most important in the early development of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma:
chapterbody 3: This is the lineage: Norlha Rinpoche - Reverend Liaoming - Living Buddha Lian-sheng.
chapterbody 4: Now, the teachings of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma will take off and spread throughout the world.
chapterbody 5: Regarding the relationship between Padmasambhava and me, I have written an account of it in my early books:
chapterbody 7: 1. Guru Rinpoche brought me to a cave in an ancient tomb for empowerments.
chapterbody 8: 2. Guru Rinpoche swallowed me with his mouth and released me through his secret chakra.
chapterbody 9: 3. Guru Rinpoche emitted brilliant lights to give me empowerments of the hundred wrathful and peaceful deities.
chapterbody 10: 4. Guru Rinpoche placed the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma into the depths of my mind using the terma (hidden treasure) method.
chapterbody 12: To be frank, when I first received the empowerments of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma from Reverend Liaoming, I did not entirely understand them.
chapterbody 14: But, Reverend Liaoming said to me, “In the future you will naturally understand. In the future you will achieve accomplishments. This is because you have a deep and profound affinity with all the various Tantrayana schools.”
chapterbody 18: One day, I placed my own hand on top of my head. I empowered myself with my own hand.
chapterbody 20: Then images started to appear, one after another:
chapterbody 22: In Nyingma, I was King Trisong Detsen.
chapterbody 23: In Kagyu, I was Precious Intent Kumara.
chapterbody 24: In Sakya, I was Biwapa.
chapterbody 25: In Gelugpa, I was Tsongkhapa. Oh heavens!
chapterbody 27: I really could not believe it. These four famous Tantrayana lineage gurus’ shadows indeed appear in the terma of my mind-stream.
chapterbody 29: The vision of my incarnations is not simple and appears more and more complicated with time, surpassing my own imagination.
chapterbody 31: Yet now my understanding of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma becomes more and more lucid.
chapterbody 33: Yamantaka Practices.
chapterbody 35: Lamdre Practices.
chapterbody 36: Nine Stages of Great Perfection.
chapterbody 38: During the time I gave the discourse on the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma, the essence of these teachings emerged one by one to the surface of my mind.
chapterbody 40: Presently, by using my hand to empower my own mind-stream, I understand the following:
chapterbody 42: The Nyingma School is more scattered.
chapterbody 44: The three masters of the Zur clan were the ones who established the Nyingma School after Padmasambhava.
chapterbody 46: There was an eminent Nyingma master Longchenpa who lived in the fourteenth century.
chapterbody 48: Jigme Lingpa was an important terma revealer of the Nyingma School.
chapterbody 50: The nine vehicles of the Nyingma tradition are:
chapterbody 52: The three Sutrayana vehicles: shravakayana, pratyekabuddhayana, and bodhisattvayana.
chapterbody 55: The three Inner Tantras: mahayoga tantra, anuyoga tantra, and atiyoga tantra.
chapterbody 57: The three Outer Tantras: kriya tantra (action tantra), charya tantra (performance tantra), and yoga tantra.
chapterbody 62: Included in the nine vehicles are:

chapterbody: student of mine got into a car accident and sustained a leg fracture. He was admitted to the hospital and had to remain bedridden while recuperating.
chapterbody 3: He was in a double-occupancy room, and the other patient was someone with upper and lower limb paralysis.
chapterbody 5: The two beds were separated only by a retractable curtain. When the curtain was sometimes open, the two patients would engage in conversation.
chapterbody 7: My student’s surname is Qian, and the paralyzed patient’s last name is Yu.
chapterbody 9: Yu had been paralyzed for over ten years. Because his older brother and sister were wealthy, they had initially paid huge sums of money sending for famous doctors to treat him. But all treatments failed. Finally, they sent him to convalesce in a hospital and spend his final years. He as well as his siblings had resigned themselves to hopelessness.
chapterbody 11: Although Yu could not move his limbs, his mind was still very lucid.
chapterbody 13: Student Qian had pasted a dharma picture of Grand Master Lu on the wall above his bed. Each day he chanted the High King Avalokitesvara Sutra and the Root Guru’s Heart Mantra.
chapterbody 15: Then a strange thing happened—
chapterbody 17: Ever since patient Qian’s admittance into the hospital, Yu would see every night a monk waltzing into the ward. Even though the door was closed, he could come in.
chapterbody 19: This monk extended his hand and placed it upon Qian’s leg fracture. After this laying of hands, he would glide away.
chapterbody 21: On one occasion, after the monk finished laying his hand on the patient with the fracture, he turned to look and smile at the paralyzed patient. Patient Yu also smiled back with goodwill.
chapterbody 23: The monk then walked over to Yu’s bed and laid his hand on his body from head to toe. When he was finished, he again left in a gliding fashion!
chapterbody 25: Soon after—
chapterbody 27: Student Qian’s bone fracture began to heal quickly, and he was soon to be discharged.
chapterbody 29: He took down the dharma picture of Grand Master Lu from above his bed to take home with him.
chapterbody 31: Patient Yu called out to him, “That dharma picture, may I have a look?”
chapterbody 33: Qian said, “This is my teacher, Grand Master Lu.”
chapterbody 35: Yu looked at the picture and said, “That was the monk who came every night to your bed to massage your fractured bone. He has also massaged my arms and legs. I saw it clearly. Take a look at my arms and legs. The cramps seem to be loosening up day by day. Please put that picture above the head of my bed, and teach me the sutra and mantra you have been chanting. I will appreciate it very much if you do that!”
chapterbody 37: Qian was a kind-hearted person. Not only did he paste the dharma picture above Yu’s bed, he also taught him the “heart mantra” and gave him a copy of the High King Avalokitesvara Sutra. Then he bade him goodbye.
chapterbody 39: Half a year later, Qian passed by the hospital, and he remembered this incident. He decided to pay a visit to the paralyzed Yu.
chapterbody 41: To his surprise, Yu also had been discharged.
chapterbody 43: The doctor told him, “That patient was a hopeless case, but his cramped legs and arms for some unknown reason started to loosen up. He chanted a mantra and sutra every day and eventually was able to move his upper and lower limbs. We could not explain it. But in the end, he actually was able to get out of bed and walk.”
chapterbody 45: So, he was discharged!
chapterbody 47: Qian asked, “Where is that dharma picture?”
chapterbody 49: The doctor replied, “He gave it to another patient!”
chapterbody 53: He saw a kind and extremely dignified elderly woman, cooking a bowl of medicine.
chapterbody 54: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: ne day, student Wu was driving his minibus on the road. When he realized the yellow light was about to change red, he slammed on the brake. Because the car slowed too quickly, his body swung to the left.
chapterbody 3: Behind him was a fast moving truck loaded with cement, and the truck driver stepped on his brake too late. The front of the truck slammed hard into the rear of the minibus.
chapterbody 5: Student Wu felt everything swirling around him. His car then skidded next to a ditch on the right side of the road. In the process, his left shoulder struck the steering wheel, and he felt sharp tearing pain through his shoulder.
chapterbody 7: This occurred at a crossroads, and a traffic policeman happened to be right there. The policeman came over to investigate.
chapterbody 9: The cement truck suffered no major damage, but the rear of student Wu’s car sustained heavy damage and all the windows were broken.
chapterbody 11: Because student Wu is a Buddhist, he was unwilling to press charges. After the policeman’s mediation, he let the whole thing pass.
chapterbody 13: But student Wu continued to feel excruciating pain in his shoulder and back.
chapterbody 14: He went for an X-ray, but it showed no fracture.
chapterbody 16: The doctor prescribed him pain killers, and he also applied Salonpas pain relief patches over the area. But, he continued to have no relief. His shoulder and back continued to cause him severe pain for two months.
chapterbody 18: He tried tui-na, a form of Chinese medical massage, and applied herbal paste over the area but still had no improvement.
chapterbody 20: Someone recommended steroid injections and assured him it would take care of the pain.
chapterbody 22: But, he thought to himself, “Steroids have side effects,” and refused. Student Wu came to Grand Master Lu for a mo-ding and simply told him, “My shoulder and back are hurting.”
chapterbody 24: I slapped my hand twice over his shoulder and back, and he left! Student Wu thought,
chapterbody 26: “The Chinese tui-na doctor spent over one and a half hours each session massaging my shoulder and back until my skin turned red and purple. He also applied cupping therapy to bring blood to the skin.”
chapterbody 28: “Today, Grand Master Lu just lightly slapped the area two times. Will this empowerment cure me?
chapterbody 30: That night, he slept soundly. In a trance-like state, he traveled to an unknown place. In short, it was a clean mountain site with beautiful vistas. He saw a kind and extremely dignified elderly woman, cooking a bowl of medicine.
chapterbody 32: Wu asked, “Why are you cooking medicine?”
chapterbody 34: The elderly woman replied, “To treat your shoulder and back pain.”
chapterbody 36: Wu asked, “How did you know about my shoulder and back pain?”
chapterbody 38: The elderly woman said, “It was that meddlesome Grand Master Lu of yours! He has asked me to prepare medicine for you.”
chapterbody 40: Wu said, “My teacher indeed cares very much for me!”
chapterbody 42: The elderly woman said, “Your teacher has a lot of free time. It is we, these earth-inhabiting gods, who have to work hard for him.”
chapterbody 44: Wu said, “Let me thank you on behalf of my teacher!”
chapterbody 46: Wu drank the medicine broth. Strangely, when he woke up, the severe pain in his shoulder and back was completely gone.
chapterbody 48: He ran to tell me of the incident.
chapterbody 50: Wu asked, “Who was that elderly woman?”
chapterbody 52: “That was the Elderly Mother of Li Shan (Pear Mountain, in Taichung, Taiwan)!”
chapterbody 55: I was offering nectar in my house, and soon all the ghosts in the neighborhood heard about it and came to visit my humble home.

chaptername: 033 Ghosts Lining up to Seek Mo-ding
chapterbody: n May 15th, 2013, I (Grand Master Lu) flew from Taiwan to my home, the Southhill Residence, in the Seattle area of the United States.
chapterbody 3: This Southhill Residence had been vacant for half a year while I was in Taiwan.
chapterbody 5: My daughter Fo-ching came once a week to pick up the mail.
chapterbody 7: My son Fo-chi occasionally came over to start the engine of my car, a Bentley, to keep the battery charged.
chapterbody 9: All other times, there was no one in the home. We did not have any cleaning staff.
chapterbody 11: Every Thursday, a gardener came to take care of the trees and flowers outside the house.
chapterbody 13: The Southhill Residence has an alarm system. If a door or window is disturbed, the alarm will sound and an alert is sent to the Police Department. Within five minutes the police will come.
chapterbody 15: A week before our return, Fo-ching had hired cleaners to sweep every room and wash the pillows, sheets, and blankets in our bedrooms.
chapterbody 17: When we arrived home, everything was clean and fresh.
chapterbody 18: As soon as I arrived home, I inspected the house. Not only was the house spotless, there was not even the presence of a single ghost.
chapterbody 20: (When I left, the ghosts also left.)
chapterbody 22: My first day back, I set up a “food-bestowing table” for nectar offering to the ghosts.
chapterbody 24: I chanted:
chapterbody 26: Big Bird Golden Garuda,
chapterbody 27: Assembly of ghosts in the wilderness,
chapterbody 28: Rakshas and haritis,
chapterbody 29: May you be filled with nectar.
chapterbody 30: Om, mu-di-li, so-ha. Om, mu-di-li, so-ha. Om, mu-di-li, so-ha.
chapterbody 32: I then used the Vajra Finger Mudra to write the seed syllable () on the surface of the water and chanted Hum Hum Hum. I then sprinkled the water outside the home.
chapterbody 34: About a week later, ghosts in the vicinity of Southhill Residence caught on to the news that I had returned.
chapterbody 36: The number of ghosts in my house grew.
chapterbody 38: They also spread the word. I was offering nectar in my house, and soon all the ghosts in the neighborhood heard about it and came to visit my humble home.
chapterbody 40: It was incredible. Numerous invisible ghosts came to live in my house.
chapterbody 42: The empty, clean Southhill Residence suddenly turned into a house full of ghosts after I started performing the “bestowal of foods” ritual to them.

chapterbody 68: In this essay, I primarily wish to explain how I came by my comprehension of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma. Also, I want to reveal the sources of my three great lineages:

chaptername: 035 The Origin of the Lineage Transmission of Great Perfection Dharma
chapterbody: ccording to my knowledge:
chapterbody 3: The Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma flowed from the perfect awareness of the Primordial Buddha Samantabhadra Tathagata. First the Primordial Buddha imprinted this teaching upon the minds of the Five Dhyani Buddhas: Vairocana Buddha, Akshobhya Buddha, Amitabha Buddha, Ratnasambhava Buddha, and Amoghasiddhi Buddha, as well as all the buddhas.
chapterbody 5: Next, the Five Dhyani Buddhas also used the mode of mind to mind transmission to pass teachings to Vajrasattva with the sambhogakaya body.
chapterbody 7: This mind direct transmission of the buddhas bypasses written or spoken language. It is like pouring milk directly into a milk bottle.
chapterbody 9: This mind to mind transmission is also known as esoteric mind transmission.
chapterbody 11: Later, Vajrasattva transformed into Manjushri, Avalokitesvara, and Vajrapani, the symbols of wisdom, compassion, and strength respectively, and used the sign transmission of the vidyadharas to transmit the lineage to devas, nagas, and yakshas.
chapterbody 13: At the Trayastrimsa Heaven in the celestial realm, Semlhag Chen was the head of the five hundred devas. After experiencing four auspicious signs in his dream, Semlhag Chen received lineage transmission from Vajrapani. He also received the empowerment and transmission of the Dzogchen tantras.
chapterbody 15: Later Semlhag Chen was born into the human realm in the land of Uddiyana as Garab Dorje.
chapterbody 17: Garab Dorje also received empowerment from Vajrapani, attained realization, and became the first human guru of Dzogchen.
chapterbody 19: Next, Garab Dorje transmitted the teachings to Manjushrimitra, Manjushrimitra transmitted the teachings to Shri Singha, and Shri Singha transmitted the teachings to Padmasambhava, Vairotsana, Vimalamitra, and Jnanasutra.
chapterbody 21: Finally, Padmasambhava entered Tibet and transmitted the lineage of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma to the Tibetan King Trisong Detsen and Yeshe Tsogyal. This kind of transmission is known as oral transmission by humans.
chapterbody 23: Thus, lineages of transmission can be categorized as:
chapterbody 25: “Mind direct transmission of the buddhas,”
chapterbody 26: “Sign transmission of the vidyadharas,”
chapterbody 27: “Oral transmission,”
chapterbody 28: “Mind transmission,” “Terma transmission,”
chapterbody 29: “Most profound pure awareness transmission.”
chapterbody 31: In the category of terma transmission, Jigme Linpa and Longchenpa have made the most contribution.
chapterbody 33: And I, Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu, have the following lineages of transmission:
chapterbody 35: 1. Oral transmission (from Reverend Liaoming)
chapterbody 36: 2. Mind transmission (from Padmasambhava)
chapterbody 37: 3. Most profound pure awareness (from the buddhas)
chapterbody 39: Additionally, terma transmission may also be included as Padmasambhava had hidden the teachings of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma in the mind-stream of King Trisong Detsen who had since undergone repeated incarnations. I only have to lay my hand on my own head to empower it, and the sadhanas and tantras of the Great Perfection Dharma will flow continuously and unceasingly to reveal themselves.
chapterbody 41: Here I would like to mention that among all the Tibetan Tantrayana schools, the Nyingma School is the most ancient. The three well known masters from the Zur clan had systematically sorted out the Nyingma scriptures.
chapterbody 43: Compared to the other three schools, Nyingma is less organized. This is because they do not have an actual leader, a centralized monastery, or a core organization. The other three schools of Gelug, Sakya, and Kagyu all have leaders at their core.
chapterbody 45: Yet, the teachings of the Nine Stages of Great Perfection Dharma rank at the top of all teachings and are the most precious.
chapterbody 47: All other dharma teachings are its side branches.
